props.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 D1 S. Cristo
South of modern Kherba Sidi el Ahmar in Algeria (BAtlas 29 G1 unlabeled)

name: Tumuli on Djebel Oulad Ali and Djebel Djerab

props.description: Multiple tumuli located on the ridges of the Djebel Oulad Ali and Djebel Djerab in Algeria, noted by Gsell (AAA, whence BAtlas).
Tombs identified in AAA to the southeast of modern Haraza (حرازة) in Algeria's Kabylia province as mapped on BAtlas 30 H4 and 31 A4.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F4 Ain bou Merzoug

name: Necropolis and Mortuary Complex of Pepi I

props.description: Located in south Saqqara, this monument and associated necropolis date to the Sixth Dynasty of ancient Egypt (Dynasty VI).
A megalithic dolmen located in Saumur, France. This is one of the largest dolmens in France.
A dolmen that is still covered by its original cairn, the Kercado tumulus was built ca. 4600 B.C. and remained in use for approximately 3,000 years.
A rock-cut relief depicting the investiture of Ardashir I, ca. A.D. 226-242. This relief is the earliest Sasanian monument at Naqš-e Rustam.
Tumulus covering a rich Celtic burial of ca. 530 BC.
A tomb that seems to imitate the famous lighthouse of Alexandria.
A rock-cut tomb dated to the second century BCE.
The Poulnabrone dolmen is a portal tomb in County Clare, Ireland, dating between 4200 and 2900 BCE.

The schola-type tomb of one Marcus Tullius is located outside the Porta Stabia of Pompeii. The decedent is thought to have been the patron of the Aedes For­tunae Augustae and was three times duovir and tribunus militum a populo.
A necropolis of ancient Caere with particularly important evidence of funerary assemblages showing the influence of the Orientalizing period.
Cemetery site near the town of Apulum containing over a thousand burials dated to the late 1st-mid-3rd century CE.
Burial site in the Northern Caucusus containing 130 burials dated to the Middle-Late Bronze Age (2200-1650 BCE).
A Roman necropolis at Brixia.

The Péré tumulus, or Pairé tumulus, is a Neolithic tumulus that dates from the fifth millennium B.C. The site is located in Prissé-la-Charrière, in Deux-Sèvres, in New Aquitaine, France. It is composed of three main megalithic structures, built at different times.
A Punic necropolis located in Los Toscanos, Spain, that was in use from the sixth to the fourth centuries BCE.
A tumulus of Macedonian or Roman date located in the hinterland of Amphipolis. The tomb was discovered in 2012 and first entered in 2014.
The tomb of Aulus Hirtius, consul in 43 B.C., was discovered beneath the northwest corner of the Palazzo della Cancelleria in Rome in 1938.
Grave Circle B is a burial complex of shaft and cist graves located 130 meters west of the Lion Gate at Mycenae. It was used for the burials of elite families between approximately 1625 and 1550 BC.
An Etruscan tumulus-type tomb at Asciano with artifactual evidence dating to the Orientalizing period.
Extensive areas of rock-cut tombs in the vicinity of Falerii Veteres (modern Civita Castellana in Italy)
Cemetery site near Lama village, Iran with almost 60 graves dating to the late 2nd-early 1st millennium CE. Most of the graves were destroyed by bulldozing in the area for a road in December 1999.
A mid-first century B.C. tomb at Tusculum.
Mycenaean tholos-type tomb located near Apodoulou in Crete.
The ruins of a first century B.C. Roman tomb along the Via Appia at Mesa di Pontinia.
A late republican to early imperial columbarium along the Via Praenestina. The columbarium is of rectangular plan and was last used during the early Flavian period.
A Roman mausoleum dating most likely to the second century.
Megalithic site in Chhattisgarh, India containing over 800 burials and several stone monuments dated to the first millennium BCE.
Burial ground in modern Omsk Oblast, Russia from the Sargat Culture with five burial mounds.

name: Temple of Amenhotep, son of Hapu

props.description: A mortuary temple located in the Theban Necropolis that was dedicated to one Amenhotep, son of Hapu, an official in the court of the 18th Dynasty pharaoh Amenhotep III. Numerous roles are attributed to Amenhotep, son of Hapu, including those of priest, scribe, and architect. Likely he received special dispensation for the construction of the mortuary temple.
Cemetery in modern Turkey's Manisa province with remains from the Early Bronze Age. This is the typesite for the Yortan archaeological culture.
Nubian Cemetery site along the Nile in use in the Middle Kingdom and the Second Intermediate Period. The site was inundated by the creation of the Aswan Dam.
Burial ground with four mounds from the early Sarmatian period. The site is located near the modern town Prokhorovka in Orenberg Oblast, Russia.

A large necropolis excavated under rescue conditions in the 20th century. Grave goods indicated a long period of use, stretching from as early as the third century CE to as late as the sixteenth. The site is located in the French commune of Laudun-l'Ardoise, near the confluences of the Tave, Céze, and Rhône rivers.
An ancient necropolis documented during rescue excavation in the early 1970s at a location known as Font-du-Buis or La Fontaine du Buis between Saze and Avingon in Gard, France.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 C2 Lansargues
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 Rognes
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E1 St-Andéol
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A3 St-Julien
The Tavşan Tepe Tümülüsü is a tumulus at Pergamon included in the UNESCO World Heritage site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".
Late Republican chamber tombs (ca. 100 B.C.) located at the intersection of the modern Via Statilia and Via di Santa Croce in Rome.
The pyramid tombs of the kings of the Kushite kingdom.
A Roman mausoleum in Chiprana, Spain, known commonly as the mausoleum of the Fabii.
A Roman chamber tomb now located on the grounds of the Policlinico Umberto I hospital in Rome.
Sanctuary of the Roman period
A Minoan necropolis located near Mount Juktas that was in use from the Early Minoan II to Late Minoan IIIC periods.
A Hellenistic and Roman period necropolis located in Messina, Sicily, in the area now referred to as "Orti della Maddalena".
An Etruscan tumulus of the fifth century BCE, the tumulus of Poggio Gaiella has been considered by some scholars to be the tomb of Porsenna.
Etruscan tumulus-type tombs of the seventh through fourth centuries B.C. located near Cortona, Italy, in località Il Mulino.
One of the few known coastal Early and Middle Bronze Age sites in Cyprus. This site is a cemetery located in modern Lapithos, Cyprus.

A Roman villa in the modern Lugnano commune of Italy's Terni province. Substantial architectural remains dating to the Roman imperial period have been excavated there, as well as a later (5th century CE?) collection of child and infant burials that gained significant media attention.
A large and rich Early Christian necropolis on the Via Sacra in Agrigento, dated to the fourth century A.D.
A Roman mausoleum of the second century A.D.

A monumental sarcophagus of archaic date was excavated in situ in 1984 at a place known today as Phaneromeni in the eastern part of modern Chiliomodi's (Greece) village territory. Its location provides evidence for the urban extent of ancient Tenea.

A monumental Roman mausoleum, originally installed over a Hellenistic cemetery, was excavated in 2017 at a site known as "Old School" to the south of modern Chiliomedio in Greece. Its location helps indicate the extent and date of cemeteries at the ancient site of Tenea.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 Tantalus Monu.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 unnamed tumulus (W Moschakome)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 unnamed tumulus (cluster W *Panda)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F3 unnamed tumulus (NE Apollonis)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (S Apollonis)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (cluster ESE Thyateira)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (3 around Gygaia L.)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (6 clusters in Korou Pedion)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F5 unnamed tumulus (E Boukolion)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 G4 unnamed tumulus (cluster SW *Odon)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 G4 unnamed tumulus (4 clusters in Sardianon Pedion/Katakekaumene)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 G5 unnamed tumulus (3 near Koloe)
Funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Amenirdis I, daughter of King Kashta.
A Roman hypogeum-type tomb in the via Teodora was reused in the construction of the tower known as Torre del Giudizio.
The Tomb of Caius Cartilius Poplicola, dating to ca. 30-20 B.C., is a monumental tomb outside the Porta Marina of Ostia. Poplicola was a prominent citizen of Ostia.
The rock-cut Catacombs of Milos date from the first to fifth centuries.

An ancient pyramid-shaped tomb built for Caius Cestius (ca. 18-12 BC), a magistrate and member of the Septemviri Epulonum. The tomb stands at the fork of the ancient Via Ostiensis and was later incorporated into the city's fortifications.

The tomb of a baker, M. Vergilius Eurysaces, built at the point where the via Labicana and the via Praenestina exit from Rome's walls to the east. The tomb dates to the later Republican period (ca. 50-20 B.C.).
The so-called mausoleum of Galba is a Roman period tomb located in Monte San Biagio Latium, Italy.
A Christian catacomb located at the tenth mile of the ancient Via Labicana.
Burial site in western Mongolia containing a stone cairn and several deer stone monuments, dated to the 8th-7th centuries BCE.
A pre-Roman and Roman period necropolis on the outskirts of Teramo.
A circular Roman mausoleum was located at the Keston Roman villa. It dates to the third century CE and is located in a cemetery to the north of the villa.

The site of Dinkha Tepe is located in the Ushnu valley of Iranian Azerbaijan. Sir Aurel Stein initially identified second-millennium BCE funereal deposits there. The University of Pennsylvania Museum, under Robert H. Dyson, jr. and Oscar White Muscarella, carried out further excavations in the late 1960s.
A burial mound dated to the fourth-third centuries BCE located northwest of the city of Stavropol'.

qid.label: Aedicula tomb of Publius Vesonius Phileros

props.description: An aedicula tomb built near the Porta Nocera ca. A.D. 25-50 by Publius Vesonius Phileros for himself, his patroness Vesonia, and his friend Marcus Orfellius Faustus. A later added inscription shows a falling out between the two friends.
A palatial complex that includes a villa originally built during the fourth century, Carranque also has significant Visigothic and Christian phases.
An unnamed Roman tomb that is located near Casale Ghella.
An ancient rock tomb located near modern İskilip in Turkey.
The so-called Royal Kurgan or Tsarskiy Kurgan dates to the fourth century BCE. The burial mound is located in present-day Kerch.
An expansive area of funereal monuments at Aksum in Ethiopia, built between the third and fourth centuries A.D.

An archaeological site in the modern Parc National de l'Isalo in southern Madagascar. The site comprises rock-cut terraces, niches, walls, and other features in a style unique in the region. Radiocarbon dating of charcoal and stylistic dating of imported ceramics place activity at the site to a period spanning the 10th - 12th centuries CE. The excavators tentatively interpret the site as part of a former necropolis made by settlers with Zoroastrian origins.
A tomb of the Hellenistic period located close to a Graeco-Siceliote center.
A Roman villa in the territory of Blera of which a cistern and a mausoleum remain.
The Ponte Rotto necropolis is associated with the Etruscan city of Vulci.
Saka necropolis with burials from the 7th-5th century BCE on the Tagisken plateau.
Tomb 3 is a rock-cut tomb with a peristyle courtyard in the "Tombs of the Kings" necropolis of Nea Paphos.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Douvanli
A major Scythian kurgan of the 5th c. BC partially excavated in 1885.
The Oguz Kurgan is the site of an elite Scythian burial dating to the fourth century BCE.
The Etruscan necropolis of Caere, a UNESCO World Heritage site.
The Monterozzi necropolis is an Etruscan necropolis located to the east of Tarquinia. The necropolis contains about 6,000 graves.
A prehistoric (Eneolithic) necropolis in mid-Tyrrhenian Italy with activity beginning in the early fourth millennium BCE. The Selvicciola necropolis is located in the valley of the Fiora River.
A Late Antique hypogeum tomb discovered in 1911 and dedicated to Trebius Iustus and Honoratia Severina.
A painted tomb located near the Porta Latina in Rome. The tomb's owner, Patronus, is identified as a Greek physician of the first century CE.
Beit She'arim National Park is the site of a large number of ancient rock-cut Jewish tombs, dating to the second century AD. The necropolis was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in 2015.
Zorats Karer is a prehistoric archaeological site of the Middle Bronze and Iron Ages located in Armenia. Over 200 rock-cut tombs and monuments have been discovered at the site.
The West Kennet Long Barrow is a chambered long barrow located near the village of Avebury in south-western England. It dates to Britain's Early Neolithic period.
A Roman tomb at Centuripe known as "La Dogana".
A tumulus at Lavinium located near the Latin federal sanctuary that has been associated with the tomb of Aeneas and/or a heroön dedicated to him.

name: Tomb of the Volumnii and Palazzone necropolis

props.description: A hypogeum-type tomb located in Ponte San Giovanni, a suburb of Perugia. The tomb is generally dated to the third century B.C.
Neolithic burial site located near the eastern bank of the Sarygamysh Lake in modern Turkmenistan, containing remains dated to the 5th-4th millennia BCE.
A necropolis at Ostia that began during the second century B.C. on the south side of the Via Ostiensis.
A Hellenistic and Roman necropolis at Agrigento (second century BC to the third century AD) that includes the Tomb of Theron.

Burial ground with 25 graves in 11 mounds located 10km Northeast of Manych, Kalmykia. These graves are largely from the Bronze Age and early Copper Age with some early Iron Age and Medieval graves also present.
The mausoleum of the three Julii brothers, ca. 40 B.C.
A prehistoric, subterranean temple; named as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1980.
Late Helladic III B tholos tomb on the west foot of the Neolithic hill settlement.
An archaeological site containing Bronze Age burials located near the confluence of the Wadi Zarqa and the Jordan River.
props.ancientName: Kataret es-Samra, Qataret es-Samra, Katarit es-Samra

props.description: Now lost, the so-called "Isis Tomb" was excavated at Vulci in the 19th century. Based on finds evidence, the tomb is traditionally dated ca. 625 to 550 B.C. Its traditionally assigned name is likely a misnomer, based on a contemporary identification of a bronze bust as representing the Egyptian goddess Isis. The subsequent back-filling of the tomb and uncareful recording at the time have caused the tomb's location to be lost to us.

The Tomba del Sileno is an Etruscan tomb at the Sovana Archaeological Park in the province of Grosseto. Dating from the third century BCE, the tomb derives its name from a bas-relief resembling the head of a Silen.
Late Bronze-early Iron Age necropolis on the Tagisken plateau used throughout the first half of the first millennium BCE.
A Christian catacomb located at the fifteenth mile of the Via Appia in use from the late third century A.D. until the Middle Ages.
Burial ground located 3.5 km west of Metaponto containing tombs dated from 600-280 BCE.
The so-called Portone necropolis is a major Hellenistic necropolis outside the walls of Volaterrae.

Ancient synagogue site and burial place of Rabbi Shamai just south of Meron, Israel. The site was occupied in the early 1st century BCE, again from the late 2nd to the late 3rd, from the early 4th century to the mid-7th century, from this point until the mid-9th century, and finally from the mid-12th century until the late-13th century.
A Hellenistic, rock-cut tomb at Norchia. The site is located within the modern municipal boundaries of Viterbo in Lazio, Italy.
Burial site on Lake Aktash on the Crimean peninsula made up of at least 12 mounds from the Scythian, Taurian, and Greek cultures.

name: Columbarium of the freedmen of Augustus

qid.label: Columbarium of the freedmen of Augustus

props.description: An early imperial columbarium tomb on the Via Appia.
A rock-cut tomb of the sixth and fifth centuries B.C.

name: Tomb of Thutmes I and Hatshepsut (KV20)

props.description: KV20 may be the earliest tomb cut in the Valley of the Kings. It was first the tomb of Thutmose I, although he was later re-interred in KV38. KV20 was adapted to accommodate both her and her father.
Theron was a fifth century B.C. tyrant of Akragas. A tomb located near the Porta Aurea of the city is traditionally referred to as being that of the tyrant.
The Pian del Vescovo is the western necropolis of Blera and perhaps the most extensive. A variety of tomb types dating to the seventh and sixth centuries BCE are attested.
A rock-cut Etruscan necropolis located at Blera.
The mausoleum of Caius Iulius Augustus Felix in Bou Arada, Tunisia.

An elite tomb at Vetulonia dating to the late eighth century BCE. Among the tomb's occupants was one Rachu Kakanas. The artifact assemblage includes numerous elite objects, among them a bronze boat that is attributed to the Nuragic culture of Sardinia.
A rock-cut tumulus of the first half of the sixth century B.C.
A tumulus-type tomb discovered in 1887 belonging to the military tribune M. Lucilius Paetus of the gens Lucilia.
A family tomb of the Cornelii Scipiones near the Porta San Sebastiano in use from the third century B.C. to the first century A.D.
The pyramid of the Middle Kingdom Pharaoh Amenemhat III.
Burial site in North Ossetia–Alania containing 58 burials associated with the Koban culture (8th-7th centuries BCE), as well as 26 medieval burials (14th century CE).
props.ancientName: Ėlʹkhotovskiĭ nekropolʹ, Ėlʹkhotovskiĭ mogilʹnik, Ėlʹkhotovskiy nekropolʹ, Ėlʹkhotovskiy mogilʹnik

props.description: The Punic tofet at Sulcis.
Pyramid and tomb of Pharaoh Amenemhat III of the Twelfth Dynasty.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 B4 no. 1 (mausoleum)

Quintus Lollius Urbicus served as a governor of Britannia between 139 and 142. His career is detailed on a pair of inscriptions from his hometown of Tiddis. His mausoleum is located a few kilometers north of Tiddis.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 A4 unnamed unlabeled (7 tumuli W Castellum Turrense)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 D3 unnamed unlabeled (tumulus near Zimizes)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 G4 unnamed unlabeled (tumulus S Calama)
The Punic necropolis at Othoca in Sardinia.
A Visigothic necropolis at Duratón, Spain, that was discovered in 1929.

Theban Tomb 320, known as the "Royal Cache," was constructed during the 18th Dynasty near Deir el-Bahari. It was used as a secondary burial site for over 50 royal and noble mummies during the New Kingdom period.
A Chalcolithic necropolis with tumulus-type tombs.
A Roman necropolis along the Via Amerina.
An area with three tombs located outside the Porta di Nola.
İkiz Tepe Tümülüsü (also Ikili Tumuli) is a tumulus near Pergamon that is included in the UNESCO World Heritage site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".
Cemetery site in southwest Cyprus lying 1.5km southwest of Souskiou Village with archaeological remains from the chalcolithic period.

A site at Ephesus that is one of several contenders in western Asia for the site of the tradition of the 'seven sleepers', individuals who sought refuge in a cave ca. 250 CE and then emerged from said cave 309 lunar years later, according to the Quran.
The necropolis of Amathus on Cyprus.

The Mastaba of Khentika at Qila ad-Babba near Balat in ad-Dakhla depression, Egypt, dates from the Sixth Dynasty of ancient Egypt. Khentika was a wealthy official and served as the governor of the Dakhla Oasis during the reign of Pharaoh Pepi II, (2284 – ca. 2216 BCE).

name: Roman necropolis at Šempeter v Savinjski Dolini
qid: Q71990907: Roman necropolis at Šempeter v Savinjski Dolini

props.description: Šempeter v Savinjski Dolini lies along the former Roman road from Aquilea to Poetovio. The necropolis, dating to the first through third centuries A.D., may be associated with anicent Celeia.
Minoan tomb in the Mesara Plain in south-central Crete dated to the Early Minoan II period (ca. 2200-2050 BCE) and in use until the Middle Minoan III period (ca. 1750-1675 BCE).

The Sixth Dynasty vizier Kagemni was buried in a large limestone mastaba at Saqqara. It is the largest mastaba in the Teti cemetery. Its relief program includes well-preserved depictions of quotidian life in ancient Egypt.

The so-called "Headless Pyramid" located at Saqqara, also known as Lepsius XXIX, has an unclear identification. It is either connected to the Fifth Dynasty pharaoh Menkauhor or pharaoh Merikare of the Tenth Dynasty.
A pyramid at Abu Sir built for the Fifth Dynasty pharaoh Neferirkare Kakai.
A Mycenaean tholos tomb located on the island of Mykonos.
A cemetery site in southern Tajikistan containing burials from the Vaksh culture (mid-late 3rd millennium BCE).
A 4th century BCE tholos tomb, embedded in a tumulus known as Chetinyova Mogila (Chetinyova Mound), located northwest of the modern Bulgarian village of Starosel.
A fourth century BC Thracian tomb near the ancient city of Seuthopolis discovered in 1944 and inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1979.
The Pyramid of Djedkare Isesi in South Saqqara was built for the Fifth Dynasty pharaoh Djedkare Isesi.
A pyramid complex dating to the Fifth Dynasty of Ancient Egypt and associated with the Egyptian pharaoh Sahure.
The Necropolis of Montessu is located near Villaperuccio, Sardinia. It is the site of about 40 domus de Janas tombs and dates to the third millennium B.C.
A large Mycenaean tholos tomb at Marathon.
A large Early Helladic cemetery excavated at the Leoforos Plataion near Marathon, Attiki.
An area of Samnite tombs near ancient Saticula.
A corridor-type tomb in the territory of Vetulonia dating to the Orientalizing period.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D3 Larnaka
An ancient place, cited: None
An ancient place, cited: None
An ancient place, cited: None
An ancient place, cited: None
An ancient place, cited: None
An ancient place, cited: None
An ancient place, cited: None
An ancient place, cited: None
A mid-seventh century BCE tumulus at Populonia with a diameter of 28 meters; part of the San Cerbone necropolis.
A Thracian tumulus located near Shipka, Bulgaria, the so-called Ostrusha mound dates to the middle of the fourth century BCE.
A communal tomb dated to 424 BCE that was once crowned by a lion.

Mausoleum Hadriani (now Castel Sant'Angelo) located on the right bank of the Tiber River was an imperial mausoleum built by Hadrian between 134 and 139 CE. It continued as a tomb for subsequent emperors through to Caracalla, who died in 217 CE. By 401 the tomb had become a fortress with Honorius incorporating it into the line of the Aurelianic walls. During Alaric's sacking of Rome in 410 the mausoleum was looted and the ash urns dispersed. Procopius describes the destruction of the tomb's bronze statuary in 537. By the fourteenth century, the mausoleum had become a papal fortress. At one point, a fortified corridor known as Passetto di Borgo connected the fortress to St. Peter's Basilica.
A major Late Bronze Age settlement occupied the site of Hala Sultan Tekke, which later became an important Sufi Islamic shrine associated with Umm Haram.
A valley in Egypt where the burials of both Queens and Princes took place from the sixteenth to the eleventh centuries B.C. (Eighteenth to the Twentieth Dynasties).
Chervony Mayak (Kherson Oblast, Ukraine) is the site of a necropolis associated with the Late Scythian culture (second and mid-third centuries CE).
The Tomb of Caecilia Agathia in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis of Pompeii.
The Tomb of Novia Amoena C. L., Lucius Iacellius Virillio, Caius Novius Lupercus G.L., Cnaeus Turranius Primus, and Cornelia Quieta in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.
An unnamed naiskos tomb located in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.
A tower-type Roman tomb located near Allifae.
Site approximately 1 km north of the modern village of Lapithos, northern Cyprus, containing several burials from the Early and Middle Bronze Age.
A rock-cut necropolis of Caere that is noted as the findspot of the Sarpedon krater of the artist Euphronios.

The "Regolini-Galassi tomb" at Caere was discovered in 1836. The well-preserved tomb and its super-elite grave assemblage dates the tomb ca. 680/676-650 BCE. The objects in the assemblage are indicative of the so-called "Orientalizing" phenomenon in Etruria.
Maes Howe is a Neolithic chambered cairn and passage grave on Mainland, Orkney, Scotland.

The collective tomb known in modern times as "La Cachette de Bab el-Gasous" at Deir el-Bahari in Egypt was discovered in the early twentieth century and its contents (the remains and funerary equipment of 153 members of the Theban elite dating to the end of the 21st dynasty) were distributed to some 90 museums around the world. The tomb extended under the "Court of Hatshepsut" from a concealed entry located north of the east end of the court and now surrounded by a low stone wall.
An archaic tumulus-type tomb at Vulci.
An early Hellenistic painted tomb at Vulci.

name: Domus de Janas on Monte Crobu

props.description: A prehistoric chamber tomb of the third millennium B.C. The 'Domus de Janas' tombs are typical of the Ozieri and Beaker cultures and are found chiefly on Sardinia.
The Library of Celsus is a library and monumental tomb in Ephesus which was constructed ca. 110-120 CE.
A pyramid built by Pharaoh Sneferu (ca. 2600 BC).
The third pyramid built by Pharaoh Sneferu.
The remains of an Archaic period structure at Mira Lanza, located in Via Luigi Pierantoni.

el-Lahun was a planned 'pyramid town' that supported a royal mortuary complex and other activities associated with the Pyramid of Senusret II, which was located less than a kilometer west of the settlement.
A Calcolithic necropolis comprised of megalithic monuments located near Portimão, Portugal.
A Paleochristian catacomb at the second mile of the via Portuensis. The catacomb is noted for containing the original tombs of Pope Anastasius I and his son Pope Innocent I.

name: Catacomb of SS. Paul and Agatha

props.description: An important paleochristian complex of Malta, dating from the fourth to the ninth centuries AD.
Exokhi is the site of an early Rhodian necropolis on the island of Rhodes in the eastern Mediterranean.
An ancient necropolis of uncertain date on the island of Rhodes.
unnamed cemetery group (on Chalke)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 F3 unnamed cemetery (by Kamiros on Rhodos)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 G3 unnamed cemetery (by Rhodos)
An unnamed ancient cemetery group included in the Barrington Atlas that is located near Ialysos on the island of Rhodos.

A monumental Hellenistic tomb located at the modern Turkish village of Belevi (11km northeast of ancient Ephesus). The tomb may have been prepared for either Antigonus Monophthalmus or Lysimachus. The Seleucid king Antiochus II Theos may have been buried there subsequently.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F4 Pontikokastro
A Roman tomb on the Via Flaminia at Grottarossa that is associated with Annia Aurelia Fadilla, the daughter of the emperor Marcus Aurelius.
A large ara type tomb located outside the Porta di Boiano.
A burial site on the banks of the Om River in western Siberia containing a number of non-contemporaneous Bronze Age tombs.
Understood to be the oldest necropolis in Alexandria that dates back to the Ptolemaic period and is located in the modern Chatby area of Alexandria.
The Catacombs of Kom el Shoqafa contain Roman period tombs, second to fourth centuries A.D.
A Scythian burial site containing burials from the fourth to second centuries B.C.; located near the Dnieper River, southeast of the modern village of Balky.
props.ancientName: Gaĭmanova Mogila, Gajmanova Mogila, Gaimanova Mogila

props.description: A Neolithic tomb.
A large, rock-cut necropolis of over 5,000 tombs dating from the thirteenth to the seventh centuries BC.

A number of monumental, rupestral tombs at ancient Amaseia (modern Amasya in Turkey) among which 5 large ones are mentioned by Strabo and attributed to the kings. Mount Harşena and the Rock-tombs of the Pontic Kings were submitted to the Tentative World Heritage Lists in 2005 by the Permanent Delegation of Turkey to UNESCO.
An Etruscan hypogeum tomb dating either to the third or the second century BCE. The tomb is located beneath the medieval church of San Manno in the comune of Perugia.
Burial ground in Kalmykia, Russia with 17 burial mounds from the Bronze Age and Early Iron Age.
A villa and a number of burials located along the Via Laurentina, dating from the fourth century BC to the second century AD.
An Etruscan necropolis with a number of tumulus-type tombs dating between the eighth and sixth centuries BC.
An Etruscan necropolis dating to the sixth century B.C.
An Old Kingdom cemetery located in the Dakhla Oasis, Balat, Egypt, that contains the tombs of royal officials who governed the area of the oasis.
A Bronze Age tholos tomb with a corbeled dome and monumental ashlar facade on the Panagitsa Hill at Mycenae.
"The Dolmen of the four Maols" is a cist and dolmen tomb in County Mayo, Ireland.
A necropolis of Pompeii located outside the so-called "Porta Vesuvio".
The so-called "Tomb of the Roaring Lions" at Veii is considered to be among the earliest, if not the earliest, known example of a painted tomb in Etruria. It is dated ca. 700-690 BCE.

An extensive Roman necropolis, with burials stretching back to at least the Hellenistic age, lies south and southwest of the so-called "Red Hall basilica" beneath the modern Turabey Mahallesi neighborhood of Bergama, Turkey.

A large (300 acres), multiperiod (Old Kingdom through late Roman) cemetery located just beyond the arable land of the eastern Fayoum in Egypt. It likely served the nearby ancient settlements of Tanis, Philadelphia, and Selia.
Necropolis in the West Bank near the modern village of Beit Ummar dated to the Middle Bronze Age.
props.ancientName: Khirbet Kūfīn, Khirbat Kūfīn, Khirbet Koufin, Kusin

props.description: Senusret II was a pharaoh of the Twelfth dynasty of ancient Egypt.

This place was created for Barrington Atlas map 43 (A2 no. 1) as a collective place representing ancient Etruscan necropoleis associated with the ancient city of Caere. While the Banditaccia necropolis is the largest and perhaps most well-known, this collective place should also include the Sorbo necropolis, the Monte Abatone necropolis, and the Greppe S. Angelo necropolis.
A villa with beginnings in the early Republican period, continuing in use to the middle Imperial period.

name: Unnamed villas and a tomb near Pons Mulvius

props.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 no. 9 (villas (2) and tomb near Pons Mulvius)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C1 no. 18 (tomb near Nomentum)

qid.label: Catacomb of Saints Gordian and Epimachus

props.description: A burial ground for the the Christian martyrs Gordianus, Epimachus, and Apronianus at the seventh mile of the Via Latina.
props.ancientName: Catacomb of Saints Gordian and Epimachus

props.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 no. 30 (tombs near Villa Adriana)
The so-called Tomb of Clodius along the Via Appia near Castel Gandolfo.
A Roman tomb along the Via Appia that belonged to one Priscilla, wife of Titus Flavius Abascanto, a freedman of the emperor Domitian.
Late Roman-Early Byzantine cemetery at the point where the Wadi Qitna met the Nile. The graves were tumuli and covered 1200 meters. The site was deluged upon the creation of the High Aswan Dam.
The Tomb of Cyrus the Great is located about 1 km southwest of the palaces at Pasargadae. It is typically dated to ca. to 559-529 B.C.
Byzantine tombs at the site belong to the sixth century when the Temple of Concordia was converted into a Christian church.
A Paleochristian catacomb located at the sixth mile of the via Portuense.
A Mycenaean tholos tomb located to the north of Margarites, Rethymno, Crete.
Morroni dei Marsi, the remains of two mausolea dating to the Roman period.
One of the necropoleis of the ancient city of Selinous, on the Buffa hill in contrada Manuzza, to the NE of the ancient city
The Great Pyramid of Khufu was built ca. 2580–2560 B.C. during the 4th dynasty of ancient Egypt. It initially stood to a height of 146.5 meters (481 feet).
The tumulus of the Lydian king Alyattes.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 Gyges

The Philopappos Monument, located on Mouseion Hill in Athens, is a funerary monument dedicated to Caius Julius Antiochus Epiphanes Philopappos, a prince of the Kingdom of Commagene, and dates after 116 CE.

The Crypta Balbi, mentioned by name only in the Regionary Catalogues, most likely refers to a vaulted passageway built in the Campus Martius by the proconsul Lucius Cornelius Balbus in 15 BC, who also dedicated a theater there in the same year.
The Pyramid of Senusret I dates to the Twelfth Dynasty of ancient Egypt. It is located at el-Lisht near the pyramid of Amenemhat I, the father of Senusret I.

The Roman Mausoleum of Villa Gordiani is a two-storey cylindrical tomb with pronaos executed in opus latericium. It fronts on the ancient via Praenestina. The rotunda has a diameter of more than 19 meters. Based on brick stamps, chronology is potentially wide and likely should be assigned anywhere in the period from Constantine I to Theodoric.
The Tumba de Servilia was constructed as a mausoleum for the Servilii family in the 1st century CE and is part of the Roman Necropolis of Carmo in Sevilla, Spain.
An elite tomb structure constructed in the late 4th to early 3rd century BCE near the Hellenistic settlement of Callatis.

The catacombs of San Sebastiano located on the Via Appia are located near the church of the same name. These hypogeal tombs emerged in the third century CE and were linked initially to the relics of the apostles Peter and Paul.
The "Tombs of the Kings" archaeological site is a Hellenistic and Roman-period necropolis associated with the city of Nea Paphos and characterized by large, rock-cut, subterranean tomb structures.

An archaeological area along the Via Ostiensis with evidence of human activity stretching from the Archaic period to the time of the Roman Empire. SIgnificant archaeological features include an extensive necropolis and a Roman bathing facility.

The so-called "Meta Romuli" was a pyramidal tomb located in the Ager Vaticanus in Rome. It likely dates to the first century BCE or the first century CE. Its extant superstructure was demolished in 1499. Remains of foundations and pavements that have been associated with the tomb were encountered in 1948-1949 during construction work in the first block of the north side of the present-day Via della Conciliazione.
The Vapheio Tholos Tomb is a Mycenaean tholos built around 1500 BC that is assumed to have held an elite burial or burials. It is best known for the two golden Minoan-style cups it housed.

The so-called "Guglia di Marcello" or "Aguglia d'Agosta" is a Roman funerary monument located along the ancient via Pompei that once connected Syracuse and Messina. It dates to the period from the first century B.C. to the first century A.D.
Tomb of Valerius Romulus (ca. AD 292/295 – 309), son of Maxentius, along the Via Appia.

name: Giants' grave of Sa Dom'è s'Orcu (Quartucciu)
qid: Q20009842: Giants' grave of Sa Dom'è s'Orcu

props.description: A "Giant's tomb" of the fourteenth and thirteenth centuries B.C.

name: Tomb of Petosiris at Tuna el-Gebel

props.description: The Tomb of Petosiris at Tuna el-Gebel dates to the late fourth century BCE. Petosiris, also called Ankhefenkhons, served as the high priest of Thoth at Hermopolis during the 28th Dynasty of ancient Egypt.
props.ancientName: Tomb of Petosiris at Tuna el-Gebel

props.description: A lost monument to Endymion seen by Pausanius near the starting area of the stadium of Olympia.
The Tomb of the Ducks at Veii is an early example of a painted Etruscan tomb. It dates to the second quarter of the seventh century BCE and was discovered in 1958 in the Riserva del Bagno necropolis.
The Grotta Campana at Veii is an Etruscan burial complex discovered by Giampietro Campana in 1843.
An Etruscan tomb located at Castellina in Chianti in Italy.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 B3 unnamed unlabeled ((1 monument, 1 cemetery))
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 B3 unnamed unlabeled ((1 monument, 1 cemetery))
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D4 unnamed unlabeled ((7 monuments, 1 cemetery, 2 walls))

An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E2 unnamed unlabeled ((4 cemeteries))

BAtlas mapped a large number of unnamed sites in map 34 gridsquare E2. The corresponding atlas directory entry cites AAA 27.28, 38, 39 without comment.

An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 84 E1 unnamed tumulus (near Tanais (3 clusters))

An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 F1 unnamed unlabeled ((3 cemeteries, 1 monument, 1 wall))
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 F1 unnamed unlabeled ((3 cemeteries, 1 monument, 1 wall))
A Roman mausoleum dating to the early fourth century CE located at Punta del Moral, Isla de Canela, Ayamonte.
props.ancientName: Mausoleo romano de Punta del Moral

props.description: A burial mound described by Pausanias as the Tomb of Theban general Epaminondas. Located near Mantineia, east of Milia.

Approximately 41 burials from a Roman cremation necropolis were excavated in 2021 at Cascina Torretta, a locality in the municipality of Calvignasco (southwest of Milan, Italy). The tombs were poorly preserved, appeared to be relatively shallow pit graves, and were likely connected to a rural settlement.
A rock-cut Etruscan tomb dating to the second half of the seventh century B.C.
An Etruscan tumulus of the seventh century B.C.
Punic necropolis on the southern coast of the Iberian Peninsula dated to the 7th century BCE.
An Etruscan necropolis with tombs dated to the archaic period located near modern Civitavecchia in Italy.
A Bronze Age necropolis located near the village of 'Ayn Samiyah in the West Bank.
An Etruscan tumulus dating to the seventh century B.C. at the locality known as La Collina in the comune of San Casciano in Val di Pesa.
The 'Pozzo Pantaleo' is a Roman mausoleum that re-used a cistern of the early Imperial period. In a third phase, the site was repurposed as a shrine dedicated to the Christian Saint Pantaleon.

qid.label: parco archeologico dei Campi Flegrei - necropoli di Cappella

props.description: The Cappella necropolis preserves a complex of underground tombs arranged along the ancient road connecting Misenum and Cumae. The earliest tombs date to the late first century BCE.
A necropolis at Falerii Veteres.

name: Post-Archaic necropolis at Poggio dei Cavallari

props.description: A necropolis located along a road that once led to the Mater Matuta sanctuary composed of approximately 50 tombs.
The Cavetta necropolis at Pantalica includes tombs and rock-cut houses of the prehistoric and later periods.

name: Kral Kaya Mezarları
qid: Q6022363: Tombs of the Kings of Pontus

props.description: Elite Hellenistic rock-cut tombs of the Pontic kingdom.
Cemetery site in Arkhangai Province, Mongolia containing 98 terrace tombs associated with the Xiongnu culture.
The New Kingdom tomb of the ancient Egyptian workman Inherkhau is located at Deir el-Medina and is part of the Theban Necropolis.
Grave Circle A encloses a royal cemetery within the walls of the citadel of Mycenae; inside it are six shaft graves constructed around 1600 BC.

name: Pimentel, Necropoli di S'Acqua Salida e di Corongiu

props.description: A necropolis of the second millennium B.C.
A Roman funerary monument of the first century B.C. that is traditionally associated with the prefect Lucius Pomponius Grecinus.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 G4 Plemmyrion
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Rocca Stefano

A brick rotunda of the fourth century AD ordered by the emperor Galerius for use as either a tomb or temple; later incorporated into the Christian Church of Agios Georgios. Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1988

name: Mausoleum of Bibi Maryam at Qalhat

props.description: The Mausoleum of Bibi Maryam at Qalhat was built for Baha al-Din Ayaz, the successor of Mahmud b. Ahmad al-Qusi al-Qalhati, the founder of the Hormuz empire which flourished from the eighth to the fourteenth century.
Necropolis in Southern Uzbekistan with late Bronze Age remains.
A hypogeum tomb of the fourth century BCE located at Canusium.
A Middle to Late Bronze Age settlement located on the hill of Megali Magoula that includes Mycenaean chamber tombs.
Late Scythian burial site on the Lower Dnieper River containing burials dated to the 3rd century BCE-2nd century CE.
Burial site in the Altai region dated to the Syanbi-Zhouzhan period (4th-5th century CE).
A hexagonal tomb, perhaps that of a Roman centurion, dating to the late second or early third century AD.
The Casale di Giovio was originally a Roman funereal monument that was re-worked in seventeenth century to assume its current form.
The Pyramid of Djedefre, a Fourth Dynasty pharaoh, at Abu Rawash.

Burial ground near the village of Qaziqulu in Azerbaijan's Tovuz district. The site contains a number of burials dated from the 10th-7th centuries BCE. The site was inundated during the construction of the Tovuzchay reservoir in 2016.
A Roman tower-type mausoleum located near Mondragone, Italy.
The "Sese Grande" or "Sese del Re" is a prehistoric tomb on the island of Pantelleria. Sesi are megalithic tomb structures of the second millennium B.C. that are connected with the Sesiote culture.
Burial site in the Minusinsk Basin (southern Siberia) containing over 50 burials dated to the 1st millennium BCE.
A cemetery used by Macedonians in the area of Pella from the Early Iron Age to the Early Hellenistic Period.
The archaeological site of Pill'è Matta preserves remains of a Punic-Roman necropolis in use from the fourth century B.C. until the fifth century A.D.

An ancient Roman brick tomb of the second century that is located in the Via Bisignano between the fifth mile of Via Appia Antica and the seventh kilometer of Via Appia Nuova. The tomb has also been known as the Temple of Salus. Its typology is comparable to that of the tomb of Publius Aelius Callistion.
An ancient mausoleum located at modern Akbou on the north slope of a prominent hill in the southwestern neighborhood of Bouzeroual (Béjaïa Province, Algeria).

name: funeral monuments of the Via Iulia Augusta

qid.label: funeral monuments of the via Giulia Augusta

props.description: A Roman necropolis located along the course of the Via Iulia Augusta in modern Albenga, Italy. The archaeological area includes the remains of at least nine tombs dating to the first and second centuries CE. These family tombs faced the ancient roadway. Nino Lamboglia carried out the first archaeological fieldwork here in the 1930s.
props.ancientName: funeral monuments of the via Giulia Augusta

props.description: Funerary complex of King Senusret III at Dahshur including a pyramid.

The so-called Martini-Marescotti Tomb is an Etruscan tomb located in the Monte Abatone necropolis of ancient Caere. The tomb was built in the late sixth or early fifth century BCE and is composed of a dromos and three funereal chambers covered by a tumulus. The tomb contained a substantial funereal deposit that included painted Attic wares.
A third to second century B.C. tomb in the hinterland of Cortona.

name: Tomb of the Nasonii
qid: Q60838778: Tomb of the the Nasonii

props.description: The tomb of the Nasonii family is an ancient Roman hypogeum-type tomb along the Via Flaminia in Grottarossa in the northern outskirts of Rome. The tomb was exposed during road works in 1674.
Funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Shepenupet I, daughter of King Osorkon III.
Pharaonic archaeological site located 8 km north of Giza, including the pyramid of King Djedefre and several necropolises.
A Scythian burial mound dated to the fourth century B.C. located near the village of Bratolyubivka in Kherson Oblast, Ukraine.
props.ancientName: Bratolyubovskii Kurgan, Bratoli͡ubovskiĭ Kurgan, Bratoljubovka Kurgan, Bratolyubivka Kurgan, Bratolyubovka Kurgan

props.description: The so-called "le Carceri Vecchie" is a first-century CE Roman mausoleum. Its original position on the Via Appia placed it outside the walls of ancient Capua.
A Bronze Age funereal mound located in Turkey's south-eastern Siirt Province.

The tomb of Publius Aelius Callistion, a freedman (libertus) of the emperor Hadrian, is colloquially known as the "Sedia del Diavolo". It is located in a piazza that derives its name from the tomb's occupant (piazza Elio Callistio) in Rome's Trieste neighborhood.
A necropolis along the Via Amerina, likely in use from the third century BC until the third century AD.
A Christian catacomb located on the via Ardeatina.
The Southwest Necropolis located at Satricum.
The so-called Tomb of Themistocles at Drapetsona.
A well-preserved 'giant's tomb' dating ca. 2000-1600 BC.
A hill located several hundred meters north of the Minoan center of Gournia, where a series of Minoan tombs were excavated beginning in the early twentieth century.
A Mycenaean tholos tomb located southeast of Amphissa, Phokida.

name: Tomb of the Kings near Enkomi

props.description: Royal tombs of the Middle Bronze Age located near Enkomi.
A necropolis in use from the Neolithic period through to the Iron Age with numerous hypogeum-type tombs.
A Roman tomb along the Via Appia.
A valley in Egypt where Pharaonic burials took place from the sixteenth to the eleventh centuries B.C. (Eighteenth to the Twentieth Dynasties).
A Roman tomb located in the archaeological area of Casinum that dates to either the first or second centuries CE.
A first-century B.C. circular funerary monument at Gaeta.
A necropolis located on the southern outskirts of Rome. The necropolis is located near a villa site along a road running through the suburbium of Rome. Excavated between 1992 and 1993.
A monumental rock-cut Etruscan tomb that dates to the later fourth century B.C.

name: Pyramid of Pepi I at Saqqarah

props.description: One of the pyramids at Saqqarah in Egypt. Pepi I was a pharaoh of the Sixth Dynasty and reigned from ca. 2332–2287 B.C.
Presumed remains of the funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Ankhnesneferibre.

The Catacomb of Balbina in the suburbium of Rome lies between the via Appia antica and the via Ardeatina in Rome's Ardeatino district. This catacomb is part of the larger Callixtian complex that includes the catacombs of Callixtus and Santi Marco e Marcelliano. The Depositio episcoporum mentions the catacomb, indicating that Pope Mark was buried "in Balbinae" in 336.
A late eighth to early seventh century B.C. hypogeum tomb on the main plateau of ancient Caere.
Necropolis in western Azerbaijan containing 46 burials dated to the Late Bronze Age/early Iron Age, the Late Antique period, and the 14th century CE.

Excavations in 2022 revealed the remains of a Roman tomb of small dimensions, likely of the columbarium type. The tomb included fragments of a sarcophagus in white marble and an inscribed funerary altar of the second century CE. The altar commemorates the life of one Valeria Laeta, daughter of Publius, who lived 13 years and 7 months. The absence of traditional pre-Christian epigraphic formulae led ministry archaeologists to speculate that the tomb is of a Christian nature.
A funeral mound of the latter eighth century BC, possibly associated with the Phrygian king Gordias.

Lisht or el-Lisht, located on the west bank of the Nile River south of Cairo, is the site of a Middle Kingdom necropolis that included both royal and ranking elite burials. Two pyramid complexes were built at the site: one for Amenemhat I and the other for Senusret I.
Chûn Quoit is a dolmen-type tomb located in Cornwall. The quoit was likely originally covered by a barrow.
Belas Knap is a Neolithic chamber tomb located on Cleeve Hill in Gloucestershire, England. The tomb is dated ca. 3800 BC.
The tomb of Senwosret III, a pharaoh of the 12th Dynasty in Egypt.
An archaeological site connected with ancient Oretum, now located adjacent to the Ermita-Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Oreto y Zuqueca.
Numerous rock-cut tombs are evident at Sutri, most dating from the Hellenistic period and later. In archaeological terms the tombs are poorly understood.
Originally the Saite tomb of Ankh-Hor, this site was reused from the 4th century BCE by the Late Dynastic/Ptolemaic period family of Pa-di-Amun-neb-nesut-tawy, a high-ranking priest of Khonsu.
An ancient site designated as the burial place of Osiris-Serapis.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B4 Nag el-Hasaya
Site of a Roman barrow cemetery
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Lamyatt

The site of Per-Ptah, meaning "House of Ptah", lies some 90 km south of Aswan and received a temple dedicated to pharaoh Ramesses II. Some of the architectural and sculptural remains of the temple were dismantled and relocated to the site of New Kalabsha in the 1960s during the construction of the Aswan High Dam.
A Roman barrow tomb on Mersea Island.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 84 E1 unnamed tumulus (near Tanais (3 clusters))
Late 18th Dynasty tomb site near the towns of Nag ed Deir and Awlad Azzaz in Egypt.
The so-called Tomb of the Winged Demons (Sovana, Italy), a third-century BC Etruscan tomb located in the Poggio Felceto necropolis.
Bronze Age cemetery near the Mycenean settlement of Midea.
An elaborate tomb located near Kazanlak, Bulgaria. The Tomb of Seuthes III, who was King of the Odrysian Kingdom from ca. 331 to ca. 300 BC, is found in the Valley of the Thracian Rulers.
A necropolis of the early Dilmun culture, dating to the second millennium B.C.
Bronze Age burial ground in east-central Kazakhstan.

Burial site near the banks of the Tartas River in Western Siberia containing over 400 burials, mostly associated with the Krotovo (Chernoozero) and Andronovo (Fedorovo) cultures and dated to the 3rd-2nd millennia BCE

The Grave of Charmylos is a third-century BCE vaulted tomb located on Kos. The site is understood as a heroön dedicated to the legendary king Charmylos. The ancient site was dismantled and partially reused in the church of Tímios Stávros.
Burial complex in the southern Ural Mountains region containing a number of Late Antique burials.
The Macedonian royal necropolis at Vergina.
The so-called Heroön at Sagalassos dominates the Upper Agora in the northwest sector of the city.
A passage grave associated with the nuragic complex at Lu Brandali near Santa Teresa Gallura.

The Great Salbyk Mound is the largest burial mound in the Khakassia region of Russia. The mastaba-like tomb, associated with the Bronze Age Tagar culture, was constructed in the 7th century BCE, and may have had an astronomical function.
A large dolmen located near Le Pouget in Languedoc, France.

name: Tomb of the sons of Sextus Pompeius Justus

qid.label: Tomb of the sons of Sextus Pompeius Justus

props.description: A tomb on the Via Appia dated ca. 50 CE.
A Roman tomb at the sixth mile of the Via Appia.
Bronze age burial site associated with Wadi Suq culture. The site is located alongside a limestone ridge in the Sir Plain east of the city of Ras al-Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates.

name: Mausoleo di San Vito at Puteoli

props.description: A mausoleum in the so-called San Vito necropolis that dates to the first century A.D.
A Roman mausoleum that is associated with Lucius Munatius Plancus.
The rock-cut tomb of the Egyptian pharaoh Tutankhamun (r. ca. 1332-1323 BC), discovered by Howard Carter in 1922.

name: Cross dyke and bowl barrow on the northern spur of Beacon Hill

props.description: A cross dyke in Burghclere, Basingstoke and Deane, England, United Kingdom.
A Roman tower-type tomb that some local traditions label as the tomb of Cnaeus Pompeius Magnus. The structure is located at the so-called Villa di Pompeo, south of Castel Gandolfo in Italy.

Loughcrew (County Meath, Ireland) is the site of a funereal landscape of the fourth millennium BCE that includes more than twenty megalithic tombs spread across four hilltop areas: Carnbane East, Carnbane West, Carrickbrack, and Patrickstown Hill.
An ancient temple of Poseidon, known today as the "Tomb of Homer", located on a height at the northern end of the Aegean island of Ios. Some funerary epigraphy has also been noted at the site.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 unnamed cemetery (on Rheneia)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 unnamed cemetery (near Oikonomos, on Paros)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A5 unnamed cemetery (on Therasia)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 unnamed cemetery (NW Kos)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 unnamed cemetery (cluster W Kos)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 unnamed cemetery (on Nisyros)
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 E5 Tatarlı

name: Necropoli del Cavone di Monte Li Santi

qid.label: Necropolis of Cavone di Monte Li Santi

props.description: A monumental, rock-cut Faliscan necropolis of Narce in use from ca. 750 BCE until the fourth century BCE.
A Bronze Age necropolis containing at least two tholos-type tombs located to the north of the Odigitria monastery, Pigaidakia, Iraklio, Crete.
A Gallo-Roman tumulus located near Incourt, Belgium.
The Trou de Billemont Tumulus is a second century AD Gallo-Roman tumulus in Belgium.
The Tumulus aux Six Frères (tumulus of the six brothers) is a Gallo-Roman burial mound at Dhuy in Belgium.
A twin Gallo-Roman burial mound located to the east of Monte Aachen, Belgium.

An elaborate, rock-cut monument at Petra built ca. 100 BC to AD 200 known as "Al-Khazneh" or "The Treasury". The Nabatean structure, originally used as a tomb, dates to the reign of Aretas IV Philopatris.
The Necropolis of Mesu ‘e Montes contains at least 18 tombs of the domus de janas type. The necropolis dates from the later Neolithic period to the middle Bronze Age.
A prehistoric necropolis at Porto Torres, Sardinia, containing at least 22 domus de ianas type tombs.

Jebel Moya is an archaeological site located in the southern part of the Gezira Plain in Sudan. The 25-acre site was the locus of the largest-known pastoralist necropolis in Africa, with more than 3,000 burials dating between 5000 BCE and 500 CE. Sir Henry Wellcome first excavated the site from 1911 to 1914.
A Neolithic ritual site that includes a passage grave dating ca. 4000-3500 BCE.
A pre-nuragic necropolis discovered in 1903.
A second-century Gallo-Roman tumulus at Chastre, Belgium.
The Tumulus of Xhendremael (known also as Li Tombe di Hên'mâl) is a Gallo-Roman burial mound near Xhendremael in Belgium. In 1933 the remains of a Roman villa were discovered in its vicinity.
The Tumulus of Avennes is a Gallo-Roman burial mound dated to the Flavian period located in Liège Braives, Belgium.
A Gallo-Roman burial mound located near Avernas-le-Bauduin, Belgium.
The site of a pre-Roman Iberian necropolis, dating sixth to fourth centuries BCE located in the municipality of Hoya-Gonzalo (Albacete, Spain).
Burial mound near the Sentelek River in Altay, Russia, containing remains associated with the Afanasievo Culture (31st-29th centuries BCE) and the Pazyryk Culture (4th-3rd centuries BCE).
Burial site in Zavkhan Province, Mongolia containing over 150 burials dated to the Xiongnu period.

A rock-cut double tomb on the northwestern slope of the Hill of the Muses in Athens, traditionally associated with Kimon. A 3rd-century AD inscription identifies the owner at that time as Zosimianos, indicating re-use of the structure.

A series of decorated early Christian tombs dating to the fourth century CE is located in the town of Sopianae, in the Roman Province of Pannonia. The Early Christian Necropolis of Pécs was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

An assemblage of four Middle Neolithic tumuli located in the territory of the commune of Tusson in the French department of Charente. The four tumuli are known colloquially as "tumulus de la Justice", "le Petit Dognon", "le Gros Dognon", and "le Vieux Breuil".

name: Dolmen de la Pierre Blanche
qid: Q3034091: Dolmen de la Pierre Blanche

props.description: The ruinous remains of a dolmen-type tomb, its tumulus no longer extant, are located on the same axis as the Tusson tumuli.
The so-called tomb of the Latin poet Virgil.

qid.label: Tomb of Gnaeus Alleius Nigidius Maius

props.description: The Tomb of Nigidius Alleius Maius was excavated in 2017. The tomb includes a 'res gestae'-style inscription in Latin that describes the life, career, and patronage of the decedent. Although the name of the decedent is no longer extant, the tomb has been linked to prominent citizen Nigidius Alleius Maius.
Burial site near modern Pottenbrunn, Austria, containing 43 burials dated to the Roman period.
Iron Age cemetery located on the eastern slope of the Kidron Valley, containing burials dated from the 9th-7th centuries BCE.

The necropolis associated with the unlocated city This (Thinis), capital city of the unified Egyptian kingdom in the Early Dynastic period. The necropolis stretches for 1.5 kilometers and contains thousands of burials from the Predynastic period through the early Coptic period, with particularly large numbers of burials from the Predynastic period (ca. 3800–3200 BCE) and the late Old Kingdom-First Intermediate Period (ca. 2300–2061 BCE).
props.ancientName: Naga ed-Deir, Naga ed-Dêr, Naj al Dayr, Naj‘ ad Dayr, Nag’ el Deir

props.description: Bronze Age burial site of the Alazani-Bedeni culture near the Alazani River in eastern Georgia. The site is dated to the first half of the 24th century BCE.
The mausoleum of Gasr Doga is a monumental, elite Libico-Punic tomb built in the Tarhuna area.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 Wadi Scetaf
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 unnamed monument (el-Amud mausolea near Wadi N'f'd)
A megalithic tomb dated ca. 1700 BC located near Waterville, County Kerry, Ireland.

Early Minoan cemetery located in Gournes, Crete, containing 37 tombs. The site was largely destroyed by the installation of irrigation pipes and the building of the Hellenic Centre for Marine Research and its associated parking lot.
Stavroupolis is the site of the largest Macedonian tomb known in Thrace.
A subterranean funerary chamber with three frescoes walls depicting animal and vegetal life, located around the third mile of the Via Appia.
A necropolis with inhumations dating to the fifth and fourth centuries B.C.
An Etruscan tomb of the seventh century B.C.
Iron age burial mound near Dnipro (Dnipropetrovsk).
Burial site in the Northern Altai region containing remains associated with the Bulan-Koby culture (2nd-5th centuries CE).

The Campo Barbarico Mausoleum is a third-century CE Roman "temple tomb" located at the fourth mile of the Via Latina. The tomb's position is immediately to the south of the archaeological area of the Tombs of the Via Latina. In 2022, additional in situ tracts of the Via Latina's pavement were excavated adjacent to the mausoleum.

Tomb VI at Qatna, known as the Royal Hypogeum, is a tomb complex located approximately 12 meters below the Royal Palace of Qatna. The tomb consists of a long corridor and 4 burial chambers, and contained approximately 20 burials.
There are three satellite pyramids, typically referred to as "Queen's pyramids", associated with the pyramid of Menkaure on the Giza plateau.
The western necropolis at Egnatia/Gnathia includes some tombs of the Messapian culture dating from the middle of the fourth to the second centuries B.C.
An Etruscan tholos tomb of the seventh century B.C. at Vetulonia.
An Etruscan tomb of the seventh century B.C.

name: Tomb of the Shields and Chairs, Caere
qid: Q101529172: Tomb of the Shields and Chairs, Caere

props.description: The so-called Tomb of the Shields and Chairs at Caere dates ca. 520 - 500 B.C. The organization of this tomb is similar to that of the Tomb of the Capitals and the Tomb of the Cornice. It has a short dromos and a large vestibule. In the vestibule are two seats carved from the tuff bedrock as well as a series of hanging shields carved in relief.

name: Tomb of the Five Chairs, Caere

props.description: An Etruscan tomb at Caere dated ca. 625-600 B.C.
A Roman necropolis located in a natural cave.
The Belevi tumulus may date to the early Hellenistic period.
A Punic necropolis near Caralis in Sardinia.
A Chalcolithic necropolis near Macomer in Sardinia.
A triangular enclosure with approximately 19 burials (both cremation and inhumation) dating to the Geometric period (ca. 750 BC - ca. 630 BC).
A necropolis of Etruscan Veii.
A Mycenaean chamber tomb at Malthi, located near the Greek village of Vasiliko.

The Strozzacapponi necropolis is an assemblage of Etruscan tombs dating from the third to the first centuries BCE. The chamber tomb is the predominant typology. The necropolis is located near Perugia in Umbria.

The modern toponym "Sepolcreto Ostiense" refers to a portion of what was once a vast ancient necropolis in Rome, located between the Rupe di San Paolo and the bend of the Tiber River. This necropolis was purportedly the place where the apostle Paul was buried and, subsequently, the Basilica of St. Paul Outside the Walls developed nearby. The archaeological evidence in the necropolis provides insight into the changing funeral customs in the second and third centuries CE, when the predominant funeral custom was gradually shifting from cremation to inhumation.

The Tomb of Gaius Vestorius Priscus is located in Pompeii's Porta Vesuvio necropolis. Gaius Vestorius Priscus was an aedile and was granted 2,000 sesterces by the town council for his tomb and funeral. The tomb is decorated with paintings depicting a funereal meal.
The Tomb of Arellia Tertulla, daughter of Numerius and wife of Veius Fronto.

Collective name for the complex of tombs and temples on the western bank of the Nile, opposite the city of Thebes/Luxor. The necropolis was used for royal and elite burials throughout the Pharaonic period.

Sepolcro dei Pancrazi is a Roman tomb on the Via Latina dated to the late first or early second century CE. It belonged to a collegium funerarium (funerary college) of the Pancratii, a name attested in two extant inscriptions. The burial chambers are well-appointed with floor mosaics and frescoed walls, as well as stucco decorations.
Burial site in the Northern Caucasus containing burials dated from the 6th-4th centuries BCE.

name: Sheikh Abd el-Qurna
qid: Q71990932: Sheikh Abd el-Qurna necropolis at Thebes

props.description: A necropolis located on the West Bank at Thebes in Upper Egypt that is now part of the archaeological area of Deir el-Bahari.
The Tomb of Lucius Sepunius Sandilianus is a Roman-period tomb, possibly of tholos typology, located in the Porta Nocera necropolis of Pompeii.

Rescue excavations in 1980 documented the remains of an unusual funerary structure dated ca. 1000 to 950 BCE that contained a double human burial alongside a horse burial. The structure's scale (50 by 13.8 m) and nature have led some scholars to identify it as a heroon.
An ancient tomb located on the ancient Via Labicana (now along the Via Casilina) that was built by Constantine I and used as the tomb for his mother, Helena.
A Picene necropolis located near Campovalano (Campli) in the province of Teramo. While more than 600 tombs were identified, there may have been as many as 20,000 originally.
A tomb of the fourth century A.D. located on the Via Latina.

The mastaba-style tomb of Ptahshepses, vizier to the Fifth Dynasty Pharaoh Nyuserre Ini. Following the intial elite burials in the Old Kingdom, the mastaba became a funerary site for new burials during the Late Period.
A Mycenaean-era cemetery located near the modern-day Achaia Clauss vineyard southeast of Patras, Greece.
The Pyramid of Khafre is the second-largest of the ancient Egyptian Pyramids at Giza and served as the tomb of the pharaoh Khafre (Chefren), of the Fourth Dynasty of ancient Egypt.
The so-called "Shore Temple" of Mahabalipuram is a granite-built tomb at the "Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram" UNESCO World Heritage Site. It dates to the eighth century CE.
Cemetery site in Arkhangai Province, Mongolia containing 483 tombs associated with the Xiongnu culture.
A single-chamber tomb carved in tufo bedrock at Cortona dates from the third and second centuries BCE. The Tomba di Mezzavia was discovered in 1950.
A Fifth Dynasty pyramid at Saqqarah.
A hypogeum tomb of the Hellenistic period in Neapolis (now Naples, Italy).
A necropolis at Abydos, Egypt, with royal tombs from the Pre-dynastic period as well as from the Early Dynastic (First and Second dynasties) period.
Area in the Altai region of extreme western Mongolia containing a number of burials and other structures from the Bronze Age (Bulan-Kobin Culture), Turkic, and Medieval periods.

An archaeological site with Iron Age funerary megaliths located in the vicinity of the modern Moonamkutty-Mangad Road in the northeastern portion of India's Kollam municipality. The site was damaged by cashewnut tree farming and is now not visible due to habitation on the site.
9th-7th centuries BCE Iron Age cemetery site near modern Eyvanki in Iran. The excavated site comprises 56 stone-lined tombs, 9 pit graves, and 1 pithos grave.

Mycenaean burial ground located approximately 8 kilometers west of Nemea containing 20 tombs dated to the 15th century BCE. All but two of the burials were looted in the 1970s, but the illicit finds were retrieved and repatriated to Greece in 1996.
Site of Romano-British inhumation and cremation cemetery.
The so-called Grotta dei Massacci located in Osteria Nuova, municipality of Frasso Sabino, Italy, is a monumental, vaulted subterranean tomb structure that likely dates to the second century CE.

The Columbarium of Caius Scribonius Menophilus is located on the grounds of the Doria Pamphili in Rome. The well-preserved columbarium dates to the late republican and early imperial periods and is noted for its wall paintings.

The Catacomb of the Nunziatella or the Catacomb of the Annunziatella is an ancient catacomb located in Rome's Ardeatina neighborhood in the via di Grotta Perfetta. The catacomb was excavated on the site of a large Republican-period necropolis and does not seem to have been connected with any Christian martyrs.
Burial ground near Yüceören Hill in Cilicia containing tombs from the Hellenistic, Roman, and Byzantine periods.
Representative location based on OpenStreetMap
The Necropolis della Casetta at Blera contains numerous dado-type tombs.
An Etruscan necropolis at Tuscania in use from the seventh until the first century B.C.
A circular, domed, apsidal subterranean structure of the Hadrianic period that is referred to as a tomb but may have had a cultic function.
A third-century AD hypogeum tomb on the Esquiline Hill in Rome with both pagan and Paleochristian iconography.
The so-called Hypogeum of the Garlands is an ancient tomb located at the tenth mile of the ancient Via Latina. The tomb dates to either the first or second century CE.
An Etruscan necropolis dating to the seventh-fifth centuries B.C. The ancient settlement associated with the site may have been Palmule.
A grouping of fourth-millennium BC Neolithic monuments located on the Cúil Irra peninsula near Sligo, Ireland.
Xiongnu tomb site near the modern village of Bayan-Adarga, Khentii, Mongolia. The site contains burials dated from the 4th century BCE to the 2nd century CE.
A cemetery at Phaleron with burials ranging in date from the Archaic to the Classical period.
The tomb of C. Publicius Bibulus, a plebeian aedile, located at Rome at the foot of the Capitoline Hill. It most likely dates to the close of the Republican period.
The Fusco necropolis is the largest and oldest burial area in Greek Syracuse. Located within contrada Canalicchio, the site was excavated by Paolo Orsi in 1893.
A monumental exedra-type tomb built at the Porta Nocera between ca. A.D 1-30 that served as the tomb for the family of Eumachia.
A necropolis for pharaohs of the Seventeenth Dynasty of ancient Egypt.
A hybrid sanctuary and necropolis sacred to Tanit and Baal.
Alyscamps is a necropolis located outside the walls of Arles, France.
The so-called "Grotta della Vipera" is the hypogeum tomb of a Roman woman named Atilia Pomptilla. It dates to the first century CE.
The place known as Priddy Nine Barrows is a collection of round barrows, dating from the Bronze Age located near Priddy in Somerset, England.
A tomb at Pompeii's Herculaneum gate that may be that of Aulus Umbricius Scaurus.
The Fatimid Cemetery in Aswan includes tombs dating from the seventh to the twelfth centuries CE. The site is included in the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Nubian Monuments from Abu Simbel to Philae".

Two Roman columbaria are located under the present via Pescara, 2. This accounts for the tomb being referred to interchangeably with two modern, street-derived toponyms, namely "via Taranto" and "via Pescara". These tombs are situated near the ancient crossroads of the via Latina and the via Labicana.

name: Great Dolmen of Comenda da Igreja
qid: Q1114081: Great Dolmen of Comenda da Igreja

props.description: A megalithic dolmen of the fourth to third millennia BC.
Cemetery site in Northern Egypt with burials from the predynastic period as well as the late Roman period.

The so-called "Sepolcro dei Veienti" is a ruined tomb of Roman imperial date. Built in opus caementicium and travertine, only the core of the rectangular structure remains. Likely located along the ancient Via Veientana, the tomb today stands in the modern Parco Papacci.

An imperial necropolis with at least 326 tombs was identified at modern Castel Malnome (Ponte Galeria) in Italy. Archaeological evidence marks these as burials belonging to working-class people of the Roman imperial period, with most graves having either no grave goods or very few. The osteoarchaeological analysis led excavators to speculate that the individuals buried here many have worked in the salt flats.
props.ancientName: necropoli imperiale di Castel Malnome (Ponte Galeria) (La)

props.description: Mound site in Khakassia containing burials associated with the Bronze Age Karasuk culture.

A modern appellation of the ancient Iron Age necropolis uncovered along the Sacra Via adjacent to the Temple of Antoninus and Faustina in April 1902. Giacomo Boni and others conducted extensive excavations at the site.
The tomb of the boy poet Quintus Sulpicius Maximus on the Via Salaria in Rome. He died in 94 CE, aged just 11 years.
A necropolis at the thirteenth kilometer of the Via Aurelia excavated between 2007 and 2011. A total of 332 tombs were identified at the site.
Uvecik Tepe or Üvecik Tepe is a tumulus in the Troad built for one Festus, a freedman of the emperor Caracalla.
A Mycenaean necropolis located on the island of Amorgos that includes several chamber tombs.
The Yığma Tepe Tumulus is the largest ancient tumulus in the vicinity of Pergamon.
The Maltepe tumulus at Pergamon is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".
Tumulus Tepe A at Pergamon is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".
A royal mausoleum, dating to the second century BCE, located at ancient Thugga (modern Dougga in Tunisia).
Burial ground containing 28 burials dated to the Etruscan period.
props.ancientName: Necropoli etrusca di San Germano (Gavorrano, GR)

name: Tomb of the Mayor of Athribis

props.description: The tomb of Psenosiris, identified in a tomb inscription as the Mayor of Athribis, is a rock-cut shaft tomb with a richly-decorated burial chamber. The tomb was constructed in the necropolis of Athribis in the middle of the first century CE (ca. 37-54 CE).

name: Madinat Hamad 3 Burial Mound Field (Dar Kulayb)

props.description: A necropolis of the early Dilmun culture, dating to the second millennium B.C.
The Costone della Folonia necropolis of Sovana.
A Hellenistic necropolis of Suana.
At Abu Sir, a cemetery of tombs from the Saite period: is located directly south of the pyramid of Neferefre.

name: Tomb of Marcus Antonius Antius Lupus

qid.label: Tomb of Marcus Antonius Antius Lupus

props.description: The Tomb of Marcus Antonius Antius Lupus is located at the third mile of the via Ostiensis.
props.ancientName: Tomb of Marcus Antonius Antius Lupus

props.description: Pyramid of King Ameny Qemau located in Dahshur.

An ancient necropolis located in the modern Parco della Serenissima that was initially discovered via construction works in 1997. The necropolis is located, in ancient terms, in a landscape between the line of the via Collatina and that of the via Praenestina. The excavations also revealed traces of first-millennium BCE agricultural activity.
The Pyramid of Sekhemkhet, also known as "The Buried Pyramid", is an unfinished step pyramid at Saqqara that was begun ca. 2645 B.C. for Sekhemkhet Djoserty.
The pyramid of the Pharaoh Khendjer (Thirteenth Dynasty) who ruled Egypt during the Second Intermediate Period (ca. 1760 B.C.)
The site of the burial of Shepseskaf, the last known pharaoh of the Fourth Dynasty of Ancient Egypt.
Bronze age burial site near the banks of the Seversky Donets.
props.ancientName: I͡Asinovskiĭ III kurgan, Iasinovskii III kurgan, Yasinovskiy III kurgan

props.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 F2 unnamed unlabeled ((1 cemetery, 1 monument, 1 aqueduct))

An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 G3 unnamed unlabeled ((1 bath, 1 cemetery))
