An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 H2 unnamed unlabeled ((4 cemeteries))
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 H2 unnamed unlabeled ((4 cemeteries))

Cave tomb near the modern village of Silat aḑ-Ḑahr containing burials from the Roman and Byzantine periods (1st-early 7th century CE). The cave is named after the Abu Halimeh family, which owned the land on which the cave is located at the time of its excavation in 1949.
An Etruscan rock-cut necropolis of San Giovenale.

Casal Bertone is named for the modern Roman neighborhood where it was discovered, about 2 km east of the Porta Maggiore. The site of Casal Bertone covers more than 3,000 square meters. The eastern portion of the site comprised a residential area that was occupied from about the first century B.C. to the fifth to sixth centuries A.D.
The ruins of the Sabaean temple dedicated to Almaqah and its associated burial ground are located near the town of Mārib, Yemen.
Necropolis on the West Bank of the Nile near Thebes containing tombs from the 18th, 25th, and 26th dynasties.
props.ancientName: Al-'Assāsīf, El-Assasif, Al-Assaif, Assasif, Asasif, El-Asasif, Al-Asasif

props.description: The "La Petrina" necropolis is associated with ancient Narce and was in use from the eighth to the third centuries BCE.
The Necropolis of San Giuseppe is located along a cut tufo road or via cava near Pitigliano.
Abusir South, located north of Saqqara, refers to an archaeological area where a large cemetery of lower-ranking officials of the Old Kingdom has been identified.
An Etruscan necropolis south of Sovicille in the province of Siena. The tombs date from the fifth century B.C. onward.

An Etruscan necropolis located near the center of Sarteano with tombs dating from the sixth to the second centuries B.C. In 2003, the fourth-century painted tomb of the 'infernal quadriga' was discovered.

The Vatican necropolis lies beneath Vatican City, specifically beneath the present St. Peter's Basilica, at depths ranging from 5 to 12 meters. The necropolis was partially exposed by means of archaeological excavations in 1940-1949. The necropolis was an open-air burial ground that included both mausolea and tombs. Notable among the documented tombs are the Tomb of the Iulii and the tomb that is conventionally believed to be that of Saint Peter (a.k.a. Peter the Apostle). The tomb of the saint, said to be located near the site of his martyrdom, provides a point of topographic orientation for the Constantinian project that came to be Old St. Peter's. The necropolis remained in use until at least 318.

A tomb of the late second century BCE originally located near the Via Ostensis in the district of Rome now known as Testaccio. In antiquity, this was the area known as the Emporium. The tomb is visible on a fragment of the Severan marble plan of Rome and it lies between the Horrea Galbana and the “Porticus Aemilia”. It was rediscovered in 1885 and reconstructed on the grounds of the municipal antiquarium on the Caelian Hill. The tomb belongs to a Servius Sulpicius Galba and is likely to be that of either the consul of 144 BCE or the consul of 108 BCE.

The Tomb of Marcus Alleius Minius is located outside the Porta Stabia of Pompeii. It is a schola-type tomb, excavated 1888-1890. The tomb carries a Latin inscription and it was buried by debris following the 62 CE earthquake.

The area known as "Sepulcra Salaria" corresponds to the current-day Piazza Fiume, a public square adjacent to the intersection of the Corso d'Italia and Via Piave. Beginning in the later republican period, this funereal area developed. A range of tomb typologies has been identified here.
A prepalatial cemetery on the island of Crete that includes three tholos tombs and one rectangular tomb.
A prehistoric necropolis in Sardinia associated with the Ozieri culture and dating to the Final Neolithic period (ca. 3500-2900 BC).
An Etruscan necropolis located to the south of the city of Orvieto with tombs ranging in date from the eighth to the third centuries B.C.
Horemheb, final ruler of the 18th dynasty, was buried in KV57.
A megalithic "giant's tomb" in proximity to a nuragic settlement.

A Chalcolithic cemetery located at modern Polgár, Hungary, with at least 156 graves. This site is the typesite for the Tiszapolgár or Tiszapolgár-Româneşti culture, an Eneolithic archaeological culture. This archaeological culture has been identified on the Great Hungarian Plain and elsewhere in eastern, central Europe. It dates ca. 4500-4000 BCE.
An important painted Etruscan tomb dating to the final quarter of the fourth century B.C.
A villa, vicus, and necropolis site in modern Puglia, Italy, located near the modern city of Gravina. The site was active from the first to fourth centuries CE.
A necropolis of Scolacium dating to the fifth through seventh centuries.
The remains of a Roman monument at Mainz, perhaps a cenotaph erected in 9 B.C.
Roman columbarium tombs in the Via del Casale Ferranti.
A painted Macedonian tomb located at Lefkadia, Imathia, Macedonia, that dates to the third century BCE.

name: Crocifisso del Tufo necropolis
qid: Q3346892: Necropoli Etrusca del Crocifisso del Tufo

props.description: A major necropolis of Volsinii that reaches its maximum extent between 550 and 450 BC. The necropolis is especially noteworthy on account of its orthogonal layout.
A Bronze Age "giant's tomb" in Sardinia.
Early Bronze Age mound located near the town of Erbaa in Northern Anatolia.

A burial area on the Black Sea coast that is associated with the settlement of Myskhako. In 1978-1979 excavations were conducted that uncovered 81 burial sites dating to the Bronze Age, the Classical period, and the Middle Ages.
A columbarium tomb located on the grounds of the Villa Wolkonsky in Rome, Italy.
The Pyramid of Menkaure is the smallest of the three main pyramids located on the Giza plateau. It marks the tomb of the fourth dynasty Egyptian Pharaoh Menkaure.
Hetepheres I was a 4th Dynasty queen of ancient Egypt (ca. 2600 B.C). She may have been a wife of the pharaoh Sneferu and the mother of Khufu.
Burial site located near the northern bank of the Tamir River in Mongolia. The site contains nearly 300 burials dated to the Xiongnu period (ca. 3rd century BCE-1st century CE).

Multi-period burial site in the northern Altai region. The bulk of the burials on the site are dated to the Pazyryk period (6th-4th centuries BCE), with additional burials from the Byikenskaya (8th-6th centuries BCE), Bulan-Kobinskaya (2nd-3rd centuries CE), and Turkic (6th-8th centuries CE) cultures.
Burial area in the northwestern portion of the Giza Necropolis containing burials dated to the Fourth-Fifth Dynasties.
Roman necropolis in the modern town of Melano, Switzerland, containing 29 burials dated to the 1st-2nd centuries CE.
A Late Imperial catacomb located in the territory of Nepet. The Christian church of San Tolomeo alle Sante Grotte developed at the site of the catacomb.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 E3 La Côte-St-André
An early Scythian burial mound located in what is now the Tuva Republic of Russia.
Unnamed cemetery in the el-Agial escarpment
At La Celsa (12.7 km of the Via Flaminia) are the remains of a cylindrical drum mausoleum that was likely once part of a larger funereal complex.
Center point of Archaic period tholos tomb of Pharsalos, used until the Hellenistic period.
props.ancientName: Tholotos taphos Pharslon, Tholotós táphos Pharsálon

props.description: The Ashen Hill Barrow Cemetery is located in Somerset, England. These barrows are the most northerly of the sites in this area. The cemetery consists of six bowl barrows and two bell barrows that are aligned on an east-to-west axis.
A group of nine Bronze Age barrows in Somerset, England.
An Etruscan necropolis at Tarquinia with more than 175 tombs.
A Middle Helladic necropolis excavated to the west of Fanos, Phthiotis, Greece.
A tower at Megara was thought to be the tomb of the legendary Kar, son of Phoroneus.
props.ancientName: Karos tou Phoroneus mnema, Karòs tou Phoroneus mnemá

props.description: The Porta Ercolano necropolis, excavated between 1763 and 1838, is the best known of Pompeii's necropoleis, in use from the middle of the first century BC until the first century AD.
The so-called Arc de Campanus is either a funerary monument or a monumental arch dating to the early Roman Imperial period.
The so-called Etruscan Pyramid of Bomarzo is a megalithic, rock-cut monument at Bomarzo. It likely dates to the late Archaic period.
The Tomb of Marcus Veius Marcellus at Pompeii.

The mid-third century CE tomb of the Ottavii was discovered beneath the Villino Cardani (now at Via della Stazione di Ottavia, 73) on 17 August 1921. Based on epigraphic evidence, the tomb was occupied by the remains of one Octavia Paolina, her father Octavius Felix, and two other relatives. The archaeological remains of a number of ancient villas are known in the area, mostly lying on property that later belonged to the Colonna family.

The Tomb of Lucius Verginius Rufus was located at ancient Alsium. Lucius Verginius Rufus (d. 97 CE) was a colleague of Nero and guardian of the younger Pliny, with the latter recording Verginius Rufus' epitaph.

A monumental burial structure in the so-called "Petosiris-Nekropole" at Tuna el-Gebel, excavated in 2021. Immediately north of the tomb, in the street area, stone basins supporting undisturbed lead coffins, as well as other associated burials, were found in situ.

A painted Etruscan tomb at Chiusi that dates ca. 170-150 BCE. The tomb has acquired a constellation of modern appellations, including "tomba della Tassinaie", "tomba della Tassinara," and "tomba della Tassinaia".
A prehistoric necropolis near punta Fenicis that remained in use through the Roman period.
Site of a 2nd millennium BCE tomb located in Qidfaʻ, Fujairah Emirate, United Arab Emirates. The site was damaged by farming in the area.

A mausoleum near Cillium dates to the second century CE. Tiberius Flavius Secundus built the family tomb, dedicating it to his father, and inscribing two poetic texts totaling some 110 lines on its facade. Located in what is now Kasserine, Tunisia.

name: Tomb of Numerius Agrestinus Equitius Pulcher

qid.label: Tomb of Numerius Agrestinus Equitius Pulcher

props.description: A schola tomb at Pompeii located near the Porta Nocera necropolis. The inscription on the tomb's hemicycle honors Numerius Agrestinus Equitius Pulcher, a Roman who had an illustrious military career, including holding the role of praefectus of the Autrigones, an Iberian tribe.
A Carthaginian necropolis on the island of Ibiza.
A prehistoric settlement and Greco-Roman era necropolis underlying the modern city of Lipari, with burials dating from the bronze age through the Roman period.
Two adjacent funerary landscapes located south of modern Senorbì on the island of Sardinia, with burials ranging from the 6th century BCE to the 6th century CE.
The Late Republican tomb of the freedman Hilarus Fuscus along the Via Appia (ca. 30 B.C.).
Theban Tomb 100 (TT100), the burial place of Rekhmire, a noble of the 18th dynasty.

Cave approximately 17km Northwest of El Maabdeh, Egypt where thousands of mummified crocodiles were found in addition to several other types of animals and human mummies as part of the Egyptian crocodile cult. All discoveries at this site were from the Ptolemaic or Roman periods.

In 1997, during work to restore and consolidate the "Blue Mosque" in modern Tabriz, Iran, an extensive Iron Age cemetery was discovered and excavated. Since 2007, the site has been partly open to the public as an open-air museum.
Deir el-Bahari is a complex of mortuary temples and tombs that constitute a part of the Theban necropolis located on the west bank of the Nile river, opposite Luxor.

TT69 is located in the Theban Necropolis on the west bank of the Nile. It served as the tomb of an Egyptian official known as Menna, among whose titles were "Overseer of Fields of Amun", and "Overseer of Fields of the Lord of the Two Lands". The tomb is generally dated to the reign of Thutmosis IV.
The Tomb of Derecia Methe is a house-type tomb located at the Porta Nocera.

name: Tomb of Annedia and Lucius Caesius

qid.label: Tomb of Annedia and Lucius Caesius

props.description: The Tomb of Annedia and Lucius Caesius is an aedicula tomb in the Porta Nocera necropolis.
C. Ennius Marsus was duovir at Saepinum.
The mastaba tomb of the vizier Mereruka at Saqqarah dates to the Sixth Dynasty of ancient Egypt.
A circular Roman mausoleum with a distinctive cupola located at the ninth mile of the Via Appia.
Early dynastic necropolis in southern Mesopotamia. The site was inundated by the creation of Lake Hamrin in 1981.
Cemetery site in northern Cyprus near Kyrenia with Bronze Age remains.
Eneolithic and early Bronze Age burial ground in the Northwest Caucasus with about 40 mounds located 3km northwest of Tsarskaya.

name: Tomb of Marcus Servilius Quartus
qid: Q97623646: Tomb of Marcus Servilius Quartus

props.description: A Roman tomb on the Via Appia dating ca. A.D. 100.
An inscription found beside the Via Appia near the tomb of Caecilia Metella in 1851 identifies a cepotaphium, i.e., a tomb in a garden, belonging to one Aur(elius) Inachus.
Also frequently referred to as "the Heroon" in archaeological literature, this structure at the site of Keryneia in Achaea was excavated in the mid-20th century.
A necropolis located to the north of ancient Falerii Veteres containing more than 67 tombs.
The Etruscan necropolis of La Mattonara near Civitavecchia, Italy.

Burial site in western Mongolia that dates to between the 8th century BCE and the 3rd century CE. Nine tombs associated with the Pazyryk Culture and 7 tombs associated with the Xianbei Culture have been identified.
Cemetery site in northern Mongolia containing 35 burials dated to the Early Xiongnu period (200-50 BCE).
Cemetery at the Al-Bass site in Tyre containing burials, funerary urns, and stelae dated to the Phoenician period (8th-6th century BCE).

The Tyre necropolis, part of the UNESCO World Heritage site of Tyre, contains a triumphal arch and hundreds of stone sarcophagi dated to the Roman and Byzantine periods. It is located adjacent to an older Phoenician burial site.

Burial site located southeast of Mount Vermion. The site was primarily used during the Archaic period (mid 6th-early 5th centuries BCE), with additional burials through the late Classical-early Hellenistic period and some additional signs of earlier human activity from the Middle Neolithic period through the Bronze Age. Rescue excavations were conducted on the site in 2000-2002 during the construction of the Egnatia Highway.
Cave tomb complex located southwest of Jerusalem containing burials dated to the 6th century BCE.
props.ancientName: Ketef Hinnom, Katef Hinom, Ketef Hinom

props.description: The oldest tomb at El-Kurru.
A vast Roman catacomb along the Via Appia that began to be used at the close of the second century A.D.
An imperial necropolis with columbarium tombs and a family tomb connected with the gens Atilia Romana.
Burial site in southern Cyprus containing burials dated to the Hellenistic and Roman periods.
Burial site in northern Iran containing over fifty burials dated to the mid-2nd millennium BCE.

Theban Tomb no. 65 (TT65), the tomb of Nebamun, dated to the reign of Hatshepsut. The tomb was reused in the Twentieth Dynasty for Imiseba, a member of the Theban Clergy. In late antiquity it (along with several of the surrounding tombs) was repurposed as an anchorite dwelling, called in modern times the "Monastery of Cyriacus."

name: Tomb of the Sempronii (Palazzo San Felice, Rome)

props.description: A late-Republican tomb located on the slope of the Quirinal Hill at Rome, now built into Palazzo San Felice (via della Dataria 21).
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 C1 unnamed unlabeled ((5 monuments, 2 cemeteries))
A rock-cut necropolis at Blera with tombs of the Hellenistic period.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D5 Torremocha
A Roman funerary monument located at modern Vilablareix, southwest of Girona, in Catalonia.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F2 no. 47 (Puységur)
A Roman tomb of one Lucius Aemilius Lupus.
A Roman mausoleum and villa of the fourth century AD in the locality known as La Sinagoga near Sádaba, Spain.
The Voudeni Cemetery is a Mycenaean cemetery in Achaea, Greece, that was used from ca. 1500-1050 BCE. The archaeological exploration of the site began in 1923 under Nikolaos Kyparissis.
Necropolis of the Jūybāri shaikhs located approximately 5 km west of Bukhara. The complex was constructed during the 16th century around the tomb of the scholar Emām Abu Bakr Saʿd.

The Mausoleum at Halicarnassus is a tomb built between 353 and 350 B.C. for Mausolus, a satrap in the Persian Empire. Antipater of Sidon identified it as one of his Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 G3 St-Jean-d’Arves
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E1 Fürst
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 H2 Aukštakiemiai, Palanga, Šernai
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 G2 Enekrogen, Kannikegård, Slamrebjerg, Slusegård
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 F2 Lilla Jored
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 G3 Lugii
The modern Polish city of Odry is the site of a major "stone circles" cemetery of the iron age Wielbark Culture.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 F2 Öremölla
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 F2 Store-Dal
A Mycenaean tholos tomb.
A necropolis of Pompeii. The eastern sector was excavated in 1886-7 and is now reinterred.
The Tomb of the freedman Marcus Lollius Lucrio and his familia at Pompeii.
North Tomb H in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.
An unnamed tomb, funeral enclosure, or perhaps ustrinum in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.
A mausoleum on the Via Appia that has been identified conventionally as that of the emperor Gallienus.

name: Tombs of the Festoons and Frontespizio

props.description: Two collocated tombs on the Via Appia - a Republican tomb belonging to the time of Sulla known as the 'Tombs of the Festoons' and a second tomb called the 'Tomb of the Title Page' by Luigi Canina who studied and restored it.
The so-called Constantinian Columbarium is actually a second-century AD tomb on the Via Appia.

name: Tomb of Gaius Rabirius Postumus Libertus and family

props.description: A tomb of the Republican period belonging to the freedman family of the Rabirii.
A megalithic tomb dating ca. 1800 BC located in Antequera (Andalusia), Spain.
A chamber tomb of the late second to early first millennium BCE located on the island of Minorca.
An early Christian necropolis with tombs dating to the fourth through sixth centuries CE in the Italian comune of Vieste.
A small pyramid of the late Third Dynasty of ancient Egypt.
A Roman tomb that was adapted for use as a medieval fortification. It is located near modern Frascati, southeast of Rome.
The Aleksandrovo tumulus or kurgan is a Thracian burial mound located near Aleksandrovo in south-eastern Bulgaria, that dates to the fourth century BC.
A cemetery (referred to collectively in Italian as "sesi") consisting of multi-burial stone cairns associated with the Bronze Age settlement at Mursia on Mediterranean island of Pantelleria.
The Tomb of Menidi is a Mycenaean tholos tomb first discovered in 1872. It is one of four such tholoi known in Attica.
A large complex of burial mounds located near the town of A'ali in central Bahrain. The burials date to the early Dilmun era, ca. 2050-1700 BCE.
A Republican-period tower tomb located in the Comune di Poli in Lazio, Italy.
A pyramid built by Pharaoh Amenemhat II.

name: Hypogeum of via Dino Compagni
qid: Q3801614: Hypogeum of via Dino Compagni

props.description: A Christian catacomb on the ancient Via Latina used during the fourth century.
Tomb TT39 in the Theban Necropolis, the burial site of Puimre (or Puyemre), an Egyptian official of the 18th Dynasty.
The Tophet of Carthage, also known as tophet de Salammbô, is a hybrid sanctuary-necropolis of Punic Carthage.
A monumental Etruscan tumulus that covers two chamber tombs that were looted in antiquity but that likely dated to the seventh and sixth centuries B.C.
Burial site in Armenia containing tombs dated to the Late Bronze-Early Iron Ages (22nd-5th centuries BCE).
Archaeological site on the Ob River in Khanty-Mansi Autonomous Okrug, Russia containing remains dated to the 6th-4th centuries BCE.
props.ancientName: Barsova Gora, Barsovoy Gore, Barsovoĭ Gore, Barsovoĭ Gora

props.description: A Gallo-Roman tumulus near Noville, Belgium.
A pair of Gallo-Roman tumuli dating to the first through third centuries AD.
The Tumulus of Havelange is a Gallo-Roman burial mound located at Havelange, Belgium.
A Gallo-Roman burial mound located near Vissoul in Belgium.

The Necropolis of Selva Miccia, located along the road leading to Vulci, contains tombs of the seventh and sixth centuries B.C., corresponding in phase to the elite structure excavated at località Le Sparne.
An ancient tomb located along the via di Terrano on the outskirts of Falerii Novi.
A dual court grave near the south shore of Strangford Lough in County Down, Northern Ireland.
A megalithic chamber tomb located in County Donegal, Ireland.
A megalithic dolmen located in Knockeen, County Waterford, Ireland.
A Gallo-Roman tumulus near Ramillies in Belgium.
A Gallo-Roman tumulus located nesr Marcinelle Charleroi in Belgium.
A pair of Gallo-Roman burial mounds are located at Libersart in Belgium. The tombs may relate to a second and third-century AD vicus in the area.
The Plattetombe (flat tomb) is a Gallo-Roman burial bound of the second century AD.
A pyramid, originally approximately 23m in height, built by the fourth dynasty pharaoh Snefru. The function of the pyramid, which seems to have lacked an interior chamber, is uncertain.

A fourth century AD structure along the Via Nomentana that was built by Constantine I as a mausoleum, originally housing the remains of his daughters Constantina and Helena. It was dedicated as a Christian church in the Middle Ages.

qid.label: Group of round barrows on Oakley Down

props.description: A Bronze Age barrow cemetery.
A Mycenaean Tholos Tomb dating to the middle of the second millennium BCE located to the north of Koronida/Komiaki on the island of Naxos in the Aegean Sea.
A large Roman necropolis in Messina, uncovered in 1914 during the works to build the modern Palazzo della Prefettura.

An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient basilica, residential buildings, and several tombs. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."
A Bronze Age megalithic gallery grave on Sardinia that belongs to the "giants' tomb" classification.
Iron age tomb where cuneiform inscription was found.

Saka burial ground in modern Issyk, Kazakhstan, containing over 40 kurgans dated from the 4th-3rd centuries BCE. The primary kurgan contained the burial of a richly-dressed individual now known as the "Golden Man."
The Porta Nocera necropolis at Pompeii.
A Roman columbarium tomb located on the Via Latina.
A Roman necropolis with 14 tombs constructed during the first and early second centuries A.D.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B3 Da u Dukhtar
An ancient necropolis located northwest of Persepolis.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 95 C3 al-Hajjar
A Roman columbarium on the Via Appia close to the Porta Latina. The columbarium was excavated in 1831 by Pietro Campana.

At this site located northwest of modern Cairo in Egypt's Giza governate, early twentieth-century excavations identified, among other finds, a Middle Kingdom necropolis with evidence of at least 14 tombs, including the tomb of one Neha, a Middle Kingdom prince.

name: Funerary chapel of Shepenupet II, Nitocris I and Mehytenweskhet

props.description: Funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Shepenupet II, daughter of King Piankhi. Enlarged by her successor, the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Nitocris I, daughter of King Psamtik I, to accommodate her cult and that of her mother Mehytenweskhet.
props.ancientName: Funerary chapel of Shepenupet II, Nitocris I and Mehytenweskhet

props.description: Minoan tomb in the Mesara Plain in south-central Crete constructed during the Early Minoan I period (ca. 3100-2650 BCE) and expanded later in the Prepalatial and Protopalatial periods (through 1700 BCE).
Burial site in the modern town of Lapithos in northern Cyprus containing burials dated to the Geometric Period.
The Saint-Michel tumulus is a megalithic funerary mound in Brittany and one of the largest such mounds in Europe.
The Brownshill Dolmen is a megalithic portal tomb built between 4000 and 3000 BC near Carlow, Ireland.

Khush Tepe, also known as Tepe Fullol, is a burial site associated with the Oxus Civilization. Discovered in 1966, the site contained gold and silver vessels dated to the Bronze Age (ca. 2600-1700 BCE).
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 10 H4 Grommern
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 10 E3 Westerwanna
A 24-meter tall tower tomb on the Via Appia at Formiae is traditionally considered as a possible location of the Tomb of Cicero.
A Roman necropolis located at Nocera Superiore which was buried by the volcanic ejecta of the Vesuvian eruption of A.D. 79.
The so-called "Street of Facades" is a necropolis that is lined with tall, rock-cut tombs that have elaborate facades.
Bougon is the site of five Neolithic barrows located in Poitou-Charentes, France. The earliest dates ca. 4800 BC.
The Campo della Fonte at Rusellae.

name: Località De Santis I at Pontecagnano

props.description: Necropolis in Pontecagnano containing fifty burials from the Orientalizing Period (725-550 BCE) and an additional six burials from the Lucanian period (4th-3rd centuries BCE).

name: S'Altare de Logula
qid: Q3992602: Tomba dei giganti di Sa Perda 'e S'Altare

props.description: The Tomb of the Giants Sa Perda 'e S'Altare is a giants' tomb located in the municipality of Birori, province of Nuoro, Sardinia.
Monte Aguzzo di Formello (Rome), the site of a tumulus along the road leading for Veii to the Ager Faliscus.
A monumental tomb built in the Campus Martius at Rome by Augustus and dedicated in 28 B.C. The mausoleum served as a family tomb for the Iulio-Claudians.
The tomb is traditionally associated with the Persian king Artaxerxes III Ochus (358-338 BC).

The Sasso Pinzuto necropolis is linked with the Etruscan culture and is located near Tuscania, Italy. The necropolis was in use from the middle of the seventh century BCE until the fifth century BCE. A new cult area was discovered at Sasso Pinzuto in 2024.
An Etruscan necropolis at Tuscania.

"I Pini di Sirolo" is the name given to the archaeological site of a Picene necropolis of the first millennium BCE in Italy's Marche region. The necropolis included a large tumulus of the sixth century BCE that included two chariots.
Roman-era cemetery in the Crimea with 332 burial structures containing 500 bodies and 20,000 artifacts. The site was destroyed with the creation of the Taurida Expressway.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 E1 Weissenfels
An ancient cemetery, located at modern Měnín in the Czech Republic.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 D2 Opatów
A Roman cemetery at modern Tarnów Opolski in Poland.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 C1 Wrocław-Zakrzów
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H3 Les Cars
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F1 Cinq-Mars-la-Pile

A multi-period burial site located in the northwest of modern Ir-Rabat, Malta. Archaeological evidence points to first use of the site in the Punic and Roman periods. A quarry of possible Roman date was used in the construction of Byzantine-period hypogea. Further modifications were made in the late Medieval (post-Muslim) period, prior to abandonment of the site.

An archaeological area at Avella includes a sector of the ancient monumental necropolis that was in use between the late Hellenistic period and the early imperial period. It lies along an extra-urban road axis. Remains of four Roman mausolea are presently visible.
The so-called tomb of Tulliola is a Roman mausoleum located at Formiae. Situated about 100 meters from the so-called tomb of Cicero, this tomb is held to be that of Cicero's father.
The largest private tomb in the Theban Necropolis, located in el-Assasif and containing the burial of a local ruler of Thebes during the 25th-26th Dynasties.
Roman mausoleum dated to the 3rd century CE. In the early Muslim period, the site was used as a shrine to the Prophet John.
props.ancientName: Maqām an-Nebī Yaḥya, Maqam en-Neby Yahya

props.description: Khentkawes I was a queen of the 4th dynasty of ancient Egypt. She may have been a daughter of the pharaoh Menkaure, and the wife of both kings Shepseskaf and Userkaf. Her tomb at Giza – LG100 – is located close to Menkaure's pyramid complex.
Burial ground near the Dilmun temple at Saar also from the Dilmun culture.
Tumulus located approximately 13km southeast of Parion. The tomb contained two burials dated to 350-300 BCE.
The remains of a Roman villa and necropolis.
The mausoleum of Baba Hatim dates to the eleventh and twelfth centuries. It is located near Imam Sahib, in Kunduz Province, Afghanistan.
An irregular standing stone bearing a fragmentary Latin inscription, the Cat Stane is likely a Neolithic monument re-used for a burial of the fifth or sixth century AD.

name: Tomb of Marcus Venerius Secundio at the Porta Sarno (Pompeii)

props.description: A tomb dating to the first century CE, excavated in 2021 in the necropolis of Porta Sarno at Pompeii.
A chamber tomb was excavated to the west of ancient Nemea in 2002 and 2003. The robbed tomb was dated to the Late Helladic IIIA2 period (ca. 1390–1315 BC BCE).
A burial ground near the modern town of Kislovodsk in the North Caucasus. The site contains 270 burials associated with the Iron Age Koban culture and the early medieval Sarmatians and Alans.
A painted hypogeum tomb of the fourth century A.D. discovered in 1923 between the via Livenza and the via Po.
The Kato Phournos Tomb is one of the nine tholos, or "beehive", tombs found around the acropolis of Mycenae. It is thought to have been constructed between 1460 and 1400 BC.
The so-called Columbarium of Constantine is a second-century tomb that was later repurposed as a mill.
A catacomb along the Via Ostiensis that contains, among other wall paintings, the earliest depiction of a bearded Jesus Christ.

qid.label: Archaeological Park of the Phlegrean Fields - so-called necropolis of San Vito

props.description: A Greek necropolis located at Puteoli.
KV34 served as the tomb of 18th dynasty Pharaoh Thutmose III.
A pyramid at Saqqara built for Unas, the final ruler of Egypt's Fifth Dynasty.

qid.label: Naveta de sa Torreta de Tramuntana - es Figueralet

props.description: The Naveta of Sa Torreta located on Menorca.

Burial complex along a stretch of over 3 km of the northern bank of the Siniukha River, a stream in the Laba River valley (Krasnodar region). The necropolis contains over 200 individual burials dated to the Middle Bronze-Early Iron Ages (3rd millennium BCE-3rd century CE).
props.ancientName: Bolʹshoĭ Petropavlovskiĭ mogilʹnik, Bolʹshoy Petropavlovskiy mogilʹnik

props.description: Predynastic and Early Dynastic burial site containing over 1,000 burials. It is the type-site of the Amratian culture.
props.ancientName: Al-ʿĀmirah, el-Amrah, el Amrah, el-ʿÂmra, el ʿÂmra

props.description: A tomb located in Rome's Campus Martius. Its remains are now submerged in the channelized waters of the Euripus canal beneath the Palazzo della Cancelleria.
A necropolis of the Roman period wherein most tombs date between the first century BC and the second century AD.
A Roman tomb of the later second century incorporated into the former Convento del Carmine in Catania, Sicily.
A Roman tomb of the fourth century C.E. built in opus listatum.
props.ancientName: sepolcro presso il Casale di Gregna

props.description: A tomb near Sesto Fiorentino, that dates to the seventh century B.C.
A tholos-type tomb dating to the seventh century B.C.
A Mycenaean necropolis with elaborate chamber tombs located north of modern Elateia, Phthiotida, Greece.
A hypogeum connected with the S. Passera complex.

name: Catacombe San Marcellino e Pietro
qid: Q1055805: Catacombs of Marcellinus and Peter

props.description: The Catacombs of Marcellinus and Peter lies some 3 km southeast of Rome along the ancient Via Labicana. They date to the fourth century A.D.

Cemetery site located on the banks of the Chigla River, near the modern village of Novaya Chigla, Voronezh Oblast. The site contains 28 burials associated with the Srubnaya Culture (Late Bronze Age 1900-1200 BCE) and the Sarmatians (1st-2nd centuries CE).
The so-called Salamis sea-fight tumulus includes the remains of an ancient tumulus and burials of the Classical period.

name: Mausoleum and Sacred area of Hecatomnus

props.description: A mausoleum and sacred precinct associated with Hecatomnus of Mylasa, who was the satrap of Caria under Artaxerxes II (404–358 BC).

This complex of ancient monuments includes the three pyramid complexes known as the Great Pyramids, the massive sculpture known as the Great Sphinx, several cemeteries, a workers' village and an industrial complex.
The so-called mausoleum of Menenius Agrippa stands along the Via Nomentana.

Excavated in the nineteenth century and only recently re-assessed, this cemetery is located east of Vulci itself, across the modern river Fiora from the ancient acropolis. The cemetery was in active use from the Villanovan period through Hellenistic times.
Burial site in the North Caucasus associated with the Alanic culture, containing nearly 100 burials dated from the 5th-12th centuries CE.
An archaeological site with nine burial mounds, including an intact royal mound (kurgan), located in the Southern Ural Region of Russia dating from the 6th-4th centuries BCE.
Qubbet el-Hawa is a site of a group of rock cut tombs known as the Princes' Tomb on the west side of the Nile, opposite Aswan. The tombs mostly date to the Old Kingdom.
A mausoleum dating to the Roman Imperial period located at Centuripe in Sicily.

name: Tombs of the Buccii and Melissaei

qid.label: Tombs of the Buccii and Melissaei

props.description: The Grave enclosure of the Buccii and the Melissaei located on the west side of Pompeii's Herculaneum Gate.
props.ancientName: Tomba dei Buccii e dei Melissaei

props.description: The pyramid of the Third Dynasty pharaoh Djoser was built at Saqqara by architect Imhotep ca. 2267-2648 B.C.
A Bronze Age kurgan site in the Stavropol region.

Bronze and Iron Age burial ground in the Baraba forest-steppe containing 75 burials of the Pakhomovka culture (late second millennium BCE), plus additional burials from the Andronovo culture (ca. 2200-900 BCE) and Sargat culture (ca. 500-250 BCE).
A Late Republican villa in Abruzzo that later became a productive estate. The archaeological remains of the villa are located in the locality of Piano S. Maria Arabona in Manoppello, Italy.
Tomb of Shah Nematollah Vali, find place for cuneiform inscription.
A seventh-century B.C. Etruscan tumulus is located near Pisa.

Cemetery site in Khakassia containing numerous burials associated with the Karasuk Culture (14th-8th centuries BCE). The site was inundated following the construction of the Krasnoyarsk Hydroelectric Power Plant.
Mycenaean chamber tombs located to the north of Pellana in Laconia.
A Jewish catacomb along the Via Appia discovered in 1859.
One of a pair of obelisk-shaped tombs located along the Wadi Nefud in Libya.
A large Scythian burial mound that dates to the seventh and sixth centuries B.C.
props.ancientName: Kurhan Perep'yatykha, Kurhan Perep'i͡atykha, Kurhan Perep'yatycha

props.description: Burial site on the east bank of the Nile (opposite Saqqara) containing over 100 burials dated to the Early Dynastic and Old Kingdom periods.
The Pyramid of Teti is a smooth-sided pyramid situated in the pyramid field at Saqqara. Teti was the first king of the Sixth Dynasty of Ancient Egypt.
The so-called "Tomba della Vaschetta" is a Roman tomb located in the Portuense necropolis.
An ancient cemetery at modern Drepano in Greece, uncovered in rescue excavations since the 1970s.
An Etruscan necropolis located on a tufaceous plateau overlooking the valley of the Fosso della Lenta. The tombs in the necropolis date between the seventh and fifth centuries BCE.
An Etruscan necropolis located in the middle of the Mignone river valley in the comune of Canale Monterano.
Rock-cut tombs located along the remains of a road dating as early as the sixth century B.C.

name: Necropolis of Santa Maria in Borraccia
qid: Q106520047: Necropolis of Santa Maria in Borraccia

props.description: A seventh- and sixth-century B.C. Etruscan necropolis located in the territory of Heba.
A circular Hellenistic tomb of the second century BC.
The tomb of the freedman of Caius Veranius Rufus and freedman Verania Clara in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.
An ancient Roman tomb located in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis of Pompeii.
Burial ground of the gens Calventii in the Porta Nocera necropolis of Pompeii.
A Late Minoan burial site just West of the Knossos Acropolis.
Tumulus near İznik Lake in Anatolia with Neolithic, Late Chalcolithic, Early Bronze Age, and Early Byzantine remains.

name: Tomb (heroon) of King Perikles of Lycia

props.description: The monumental tomb of Perikles was erected in Limyra. The tomb takes the form of an Ionic Greek temple.

Lugnano in Teverina is a comune located in the Province of Terni in the modern Italian region of Umbria. It lies about 60 km south of Perugia and 25 km west of Terni. Archaeological investigation has documented important examples of villa culture as well as Late Antique funereal evidence.
An ancient settlement 30 km north of Rome that was excavated from 1986-90. An imperial villa existed on the site up until the third century AD.

Quinto Fiorentino is a place located in the plain of the Arno River to the west of Florence. Its location corresponds to the fifth mile of the Roman road. The site is especially known for two Etruscan tholos-type tombs a thòlos, one of which is the Tomba della Mula, and the other the Tomba della Montagnola.
A Roman tomb that supports a Medieval farmhouse (casale) at the sixth mile of the Via Appia. The tomb originally dates ca. 30 BC; it was a large, circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 35 m.
A ruined Roman mausoleum on the grounds of the Caffarella regional park. The so-called Cessati Spiriti mausoleum was once clad in travertine.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 La Colombella

The Domus Marmeniae is a villa located on the Via Appia near the fourth milestone. The villa grounds hosted a pagan necropolis and tradition holds that the matrona, Marmenia, relocated the body of St. Urban to a mausoleum built on the grounds.
A villa attributed to Cnaeus Pompeius at Albano Laziale.
A large catacomb on the Via Appia south of Rome notable especially for its so-called 'Crypt of the Popes'. The catacomb covers approximately 15 hectares.
S. Ermete is the site of a Paleochristian basilica and catacomb; the catacombs are also referred to as the Catacombs of Bassilla, and both names occur in the documentary sources.
An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 S. Felicita
A catacomb connected with the Christian Saint Hippolytus of Rome along the Via Tiburtina.
A Christian catacomb along the Via Flaminia dedicated to S. Valentino.

A tomb on the Via Appia south of Rome belonging to Caecilia Metella, a daughter of Metellus Creticus. She was born ca. 100 BC. In 1303, the tomb was incorporated into the Castrum Caetani, serving as the main tower of the fortification.

name: Tomba degli Orazi e Curiazi
qid: Q55083628: via Appia - Grande sepolcro a cinque coni, attribuito agli Orazi e Curiazi

props.description: A Roman tomb near Albano Laziale connected in tradition with the story of the contest between the Horatii and Curiatii.
An imperial Roman tomb of Publius Vibius Marianus, erroneously identified as the Tomba di Nerone (Tomb of Nero) by G. B. Piranesi.

The tomb of the extended family (familia) of Lucius Arruntius (consul 6 CE) was located in the Viale della Principessa Margherita, about 100 meters from the Porta Maggiore.

A segment of an imperial necropolis preserved along a portion of a consular road on the Vatican Hill. The Via Triumphalis necropolis was uncovered during the construction of a parking structure in the Via del Pellegrino.
An extensive necropolis of the Kerma culture located on Sai Island.

props.description: The Mausoleum of the Atilii is a Roman mausoleum dating from the second or third centuries A.D. The ruins are located in the municipal territory of Sádaba, Aragon, eastern Spain.

An Etruscan necropolis located about 2 km south-west of Tuscania that overlooks the valley of the Marta river. The necropolis takes its name from the adjacent Church of the Madonna dell’Olivo.

A Roman tower tomb located northeast of Villajoyosa, Spain. It is also known as the Torre de Hércules.

The Tomb of the Stronnii at Pompeii is a monumental altar tomb that is flanked by lions carved in tuff.

The Maltepe Tumulus is located in Manole, Bulgaria. The tumulus measures 125 – 130 m in diameter and some 21 m in height.

The Catacombs "Ad Decimum" are located at the tenth mile of the ancient Via Latina. This position corresponds roughly to the sixth kilometer of the present-day via Anagnina. The catacomb is well-preserved with roughly one thousand graves dating from the second to the fifth centuries CE.
A Neolithic mound site on the island of Anglesey.
A burial site in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, containing over 500 burials dated to the 5th-9th centuries CE.
